articles.headline: New CAFE norms may mean for India
articles.summary: Government's draft CAFE 3 norms, effective April 2027, will force carmakers to launch more electric, hybrid, flex-fuel, and CNG vehicles. This could increase overall vehicle prices but also offer more clean-tech options. Non-compliance incurs significant penalties. Similar norms are planned for two-wheelers.

articles.headline: How India compares with EM peers in FDI
articles.summary: India saw a strong 44% rebound in foreign direct investment (FDI) to $38.9 billion in 2025, recovering from two years of decline but remaining below pre-pandemic levels. This positions India among the strongest performers in FDI rebound compared to other major emerging markets.

articles.headline: Rural inflation rising at fast pace
articles.summary: India's retail inflation surpassed RBI's 4% target in June, driven by rural inflation (4.74%) outpacing urban (3.92%). Rising food prices and non-food items, impacted by the West Asia war and patchy rainfall, are eroding rural real earnings and signalling broader price increases across categories like personal care and clothing.

articles.headline: UltraTech beats Street in Q1, digests fuel costs
articles.summary: UltraTech Cement reported a 17% y-o-y net profit rise to ₹2,599 crore in Q1 FY27, exceeding estimates despite sharp fuel cost increases. Robust demand, double-digit volume growth, and contributions from acquired businesses helped absorb higher power, fuel, and freight costs, increasing operating Ebitda per tonne to ₹1,214.

articles.headline: Overseas Indians park $17.41 billion in FCNR deposits
articles.summary: Non-resident Indians (NRIs) deposited $17.41 billion into Indian banks via FCNR deposits under a new RBI incentive scheme by July 17. The dollar swap facility received $20.72 billion in total, with FCNR-B deposits being the largest share, marking a strong start to attracting dollars and boosting the rupee.

articles.headline: Crackdowns fuel white-collar lawyer demand
articles.summary: India's top law firms are expanding white-collar crime and investigations practices due to accelerated enforcement by agencies like ED and Income Tax. Companies are increasingly commissioning internal probes and compliance reviews to mitigate risks before formal investigations. Practice has grown 30% over three years.

articles.headline: Range-extended EVs live dual lives as EVs, hybrids
articles.summary: The Centre proposes to give range-extended electric vehicles (REEVs) the same fuel-efficiency incentives as battery electric vehicles (BEVs) under CAFE III norms, despite classifying them as hybrids. This apparent inconsistency has reignited debate on REEV regulation and incentives, with some experts questioning the impact on zero-emission goals.

articles.headline: GLP-1s for weight loss lead to anorexia care
articles.summary: Individuals using GLP-1 medications like Mounjaro for weight loss have developed anorexia, highlighting a dangerous downside to the "miracle drugs." With 13 million Americans on GLP-1s, therapists report a rise in disordered eating triggered by their use, posing a serious concern given anorexia's high mortality rate.

articles.headline: Basmati acreage may rise despite monsoon risks
articles.summary: India's basmati rice acreage is expected to increase this kharif season due to strong prices and export demand, encouraging a shift from conventional paddy. This is despite uneven monsoon rains delaying overall paddy sowing. Farmers are expanding short-duration basmati varieties, with experts watching for rainfall improvements.

articles.headline: India ships in $50 bn worth of oil in June quarter
articles.summary: India's crude oil import bill surged 60% year-on-year to nearly $50 billion in Q1, despite a fall in import volume. The rise is attributed to the end of West Asia war-related discounts and a general increase in oil prices, putting pressure on India's energy costs amidst a weakening rupee.

articles.headline: Centre to tighten tobacco collection, testing rules
articles.summary: The Union government is tightening rules for collecting and testing tobacco products, standardizing the process for sample integrity. States will collect samples from retail shops and manufacturing warehouses, with rules specifying sample sizes and secure transport to labs. The move aims to combat tobacco's health impact.

articles.headline: Centre to release onion buffer to tame festive prices
articles.summary: The Centre plans a calibrated release of onions from its buffer stock starting September to bridge seasonal supply gaps before fresh kharif arrivals, aiming to stabilize prices during the festive and wedding season. Onion prices have firmed up, with some retail prices exceeding ₹50/kg, prompting government intervention.

articles.headline: Rising token bills drive IT clients to rethink AI models
articles.summary: As companies scale AI adoption, rising token consumption costs are prompting IT clients to rethink AI model deployment. Firms like TCS, HCLTech, and Wipro report clients are shifting routine work to smaller models and reserving larger ones for complex tasks to contain spiralling token bills, even as unit costs decrease.

articles.headline: Veriqus raises ₹387 cr in latest round
articles.summary: Veriqus Group, a wealth and asset management platform for high net worth individuals and family offices, raised approximately ₹387 crore in a funding round led by Norwest Venture Partners. The firm aims to combine advisory expertise with technology-enabled execution across portfolio analytics and client reporting.

articles.headline: Skylark Labs to raise $150 mn, eyes unicorn status
articles.summary: California-headquartered physical AI startup Skylark Labs is in talks to raise $100-$150 million, potentially valuing the company at over $1 billion. The firm, which builds self-learning AI for defence, border security, and public safety, expects 70% of the funding to be committed from investors including Bommie Capital.

articles.headline: Can ICICI hold lead over HDFC?
articles.summary: ICICI Bank outperformed HDFC Bank in Q1 FY27 with stronger loan growth (20% vs 16%), wider net interest margin (4.36%), and improved return on assets (2.5%). HDFC faces merger impact, high-cost borrowings, and governance concerns, though it expects NIM to narrow over time with cheaper funding and retail loan growth.

articles.headline: Havells’ margin recovery hinges on price hikes, A&P thrift
articles.summary: Havells India's Q1 FY27 revenue grew almost 20% to ₹6,518 crore, but Ebitda margin fell to 7.2% due to doubled advertising & sales promotion (A&P) expenses. Management expects A&P to normalize, supporting earnings growth alongside staggered price hikes. Renewables business surged 236%, but Lloyd remained loss-making.

articles.headline: Demat accounts boom, but trading lags
articles.summary: India's demat accounts reached 232 million, but active participation fell to 19.1% in Q1 FY27 from 26.32% in FY24. The slowdown is attributed to weaker market returns, tighter derivatives regulations, and geopolitical tensions. Regulatory measures to curb speculation in derivatives have significantly reduced trading activity.

articles.headline: War lifts defence stocks; hard test ahead
articles.summary: India's defence sector rally continues in 2026, with the Nifty Defence index up 20% year-to-date, fueled by West Asia conflict and self-reliance push. With valuations high, the debate shifts from new orders to execution capabilities. Analysts suggest future returns will rely on earnings growth more than valuation re-rating.

articles.headline: US expands bombing, Iran hits Gulf states
articles.summary: The US conducted new airstrikes targeting Iran, which responded by attacking Bahrain and Kuwait, home to US military assets. This escalation, following the death of an American service member, has pushed both sides closer to all-out war, causing a near halt in Strait of Hormuz shipping and driving oil prices above $90/barrel.

articles.headline: Short bets against US stocks hit record
articles.summary: Bearish bets against US stocks reached a record high, with short interest in S&P 500 stocks at 3.79% of free float, reflecting anxiety despite market gains. The trend is driven by concerns over AI spending, China competition, and tighter derivatives regulations, causing a wait-and-watch approach among traders.

articles.headline: DeepSeek founder’s fund slumps as AI rout hits China quants
articles.summary: China's quantitative hedge funds experienced steep drawdowns last week, with DeepSeek founder Liang Wen-feng's fund slumping 15.7%. The market rout followed a global selloff in chip stocks, intensifying worries about an AI bubble and creating a rare headwind for quant strategies amid high market volatility.

articles.headline: SBI Funds pays slim IPO fees
articles.summary: SBI Funds Management's $1 billion IPO will pay its nine investment banks only ₹46.25 million ($479,000) in fees, significantly lower than comparable deals. Several Wall Street firms declined roles due to these unusually low fees, reflecting a stark difference from rival ICICI Prudential's 2025 offering.

articles.headline: Godrej opens India’s first palm oil complex
articles.summary: Godrej Agrovet inaugurated India's first Integrated Oil Palm Complex in Khammam, Telangana, consolidating the entire oil palm value chain. The ₹300 crore investment includes a mill with 30 tonnes/hour capacity, supporting plans to increase oil palm plantation in Telangana to 30,000 hectares by 2030.

articles.headline: CJP leaders say govt assures discussions on demands
articles.summary: The youth-led Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) movement, protesting exam paper leaks, marched on Parliament, prompting Health Minister J.P. Nadda to promise discussions on their demands. The movement, galvanized by activist Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike, seeks the education minister's resignation and compensation for student suicides.

articles.headline: IL&FS group repays debt by June 2026
articles.summary: The debt-ridden IL&FS group repaid ₹50,387 crore to creditors by June 2026, reaching 82.6% of its ₹61,000 crore target for overall debt resolution. This repayment covers 88.4% of its total debt of ₹91,000 crore owed to public sector banks and financial institutions, achieved through asset resolution and interim distribution.

articles.headline: Rupee falls 6 paise to close 96.36 against dollar
articles.summary: The rupee depreciated 6 paise to 96.36 against the US dollar, driven by risk aversion in global markets and surging crude oil prices. Escalating US-Iran conflict and rising US Treasury yields weighed on the currency, which opened at 96.53 and traded between 96.35 and 96.53 during the day.

articles.headline: Exim Bank plans $300 million bond sale via RBI’s scheme
articles.summary: Export-Import Bank of India (Exim) plans to raise $300 million through three-year floating-rate dollar bonds via RBI's subsidized hedging facility. This makes Exim the latest borrower to tap the scheme, joining Power Finance Corp, HDFC Bank, and Axis Bank, which have already raised $2.15 billion.

articles.headline: India-Tibet barter trade to resume
articles.summary: The annual barter trade between Indian and Tibetan traders, suspended since 2019 due to covid, is set to resume on August 1 via Lipulekh Pass. Indian traders will enter Purang, Tibet, for goods like rock sugar and cosmetics, bringing back wool and pashmina, despite concerns over the delayed start and limited trade window.

articles.headline: Vikram-1 success draws global attention
articles.summary: Skyroot Aerospace founder Pawan Kumar Chandana states the successful Vikram-1 orbital rocket launch, India's first private one, strengthens the country's global credibility in space technology. The firm aims for another Vikram-1 launch this year and Vikram-2 by late next year, accelerating scale despite capital challenges.

articles.headline: Westside is Trent’s new growth engine
articles.summary: Tata Group's Trent Ltd plans to double Westside store openings to 100 annually, aiming to reinvigorate growth amidst slowing Zudio performance and fierce competition. The premium clothing brand seeks expansion in northeastern states and existing metros, focusing on a "fashion spin" to drive impulsive shopping.

articles.headline: Nuvoco looks beyond East with Gujarat expansion
articles.summary: Nuvoco Vistas Corp. plans to expand significantly in Gujarat after acquiring Vadraj Cement, aiming for 5 million tonnes of annual sales by 2030 and a 12% market share. The move is part of a strategy to reduce dependence on eastern India and grow in regions with limestone reserves like Rajasthan and Karnataka, avoiding South India for now.

articles.headline: TV industry eyes alternative metrics as BARC ratings stop
articles.summary: India's broadcasting industry is seeking alternative metrics for advertising operations after BARC suspended TV ratings for news and non-news genres. Broadcasters and agencies are relying on historical data, DTH/cable data, and OTT analytics, but fear pricing pressure and budget shifts to digital if the suspension continues.

articles.headline: Pilot bodies split as Gulf conflict brings back safety fears
articles.summary: As US-Iran hostilities escalate, a section of Indian pilots urges suspending flights to UAE due to safety concerns over missile threats and GPS interference. However, India's largest pilots' association opposes this, arguing Indian carriers shouldn't lose out when Middle East airlines continue operations in the war-hit region.

articles.headline: Why Sterling Holidays is moving beyond holiday memberships
articles.summary: Sterling Holiday Resorts is phasing out its vacation ownership business, which it stopped selling 2.5 years ago, to build a conventional hotel business. Its 80,000 legacy members now provide occupancy cushion. The move is ahead of its demerger from Thomas Cook India and a listing, aiming for 95 properties by 2027.

articles.headline: Inside Kerala’s global ingredients powerhouse
articles.summary: Synthite Industries, a Kochi-based company founded in 1972, has become the world’s largest producer of spice oleoresins, with $500 million in revenue. It's now transforming to expand beyond commodity extracts into higher-value natural solutions, global manufacturing, strategic acquisitions, and aims for $1 billion by 2030.

articles.headline: Multi-lakh coaching burden parents cannot afford
articles.summary: Parents face a multi-lakh coaching burden for competitive exams, with costs of ₹2-4 lakh per child before college. Education inflation at 10-12% strains finances, often leading to dipping into savings or taking loans. Experts advise a two-bucket strategy: liquid investments for coaching and equity-heavy for degree costs.

articles.headline: Final act: hospitality’s cash registers play into extra time
articles.summary: The Fifa World Cup final boosted India's hospitality sector, with restaurants, bars, hotels, and food delivery apps experiencing a surge in late-night business. Several states extended operating hours, leading to a 28-30% jump in late-night sales for some, demonstrating strong consumer demand for live sports viewing.

articles.headline: OTT film takedowns leave platforms counting cost
articles.summary: Recent OTT film takedowns, like "Satluj" from ZEE5, highlight significant financial losses for platforms. They lose millions in sunk licence fees and unrecoverable marketing spend, face tougher contracts, and increased scrutiny for sensitive content. Experts warn of potential stricter government rules and self-censorship.

articles.headline: Modern yoga goes beyond the studio
articles.summary: Yoga is evolving beyond traditional studios to cater to modern lifestyles, with specialized sessions like SUP Yoga on paddleboards, classes for perimenopause, and children's yoga. Workshops are held in diverse locations and online, making ancient practices convenient and personalized for a new generation.

articles.headline: Yoga for runners: Training the mind as much as the body
articles.summary: Specialized yoga sessions are helping runners build stronger bodies and mental endurance, complementing physical training by managing fatigue and improving recovery. Practices like Bhramari pranayama calm the nervous system, improve circulation, and reduce stress, addressing both physical and mental challenges of endurance sports.

articles.headline: How to train your body for the first pull-up
articles.summary: Achieving the first pull-up is challenging, engaging multiple upper body muscles. Tips include using boxes, chairs, and resistance bands for assistance; practicing scapular dead hangs to build mind-muscle connection and grip strength; doing inverted rows to engage lats; and performing isometric holds to build strength progressively.

articles.headline: Eating disorders in the age of endless scrolling
articles.summary: Public understanding of eating disorders, complex conditions shaped by psychological and social factors, remains rudimentary in India. Social media creates unrealistic beauty standards, and GLP-1 weight-loss drugs normalize cosmetic use, forming a dangerous cycle. Experts advocate "media self-care" and increased mental-health professional engagement online.

articles.headline: Chaos on streets echoes in House
articles.summary: Protests against the new Agnipath military recruitment scheme continued on Monday, leading to widespread disruptions, damage to public property, and protests by hundreds of thousands of youths across several states.  The Union government assured that the scheme would bring better opportunities and disciplined youth into the armed forces.

articles.headline: Wanchuk Digs in, Announces Cong's 'Coordination'
articles.summary: The Congress has decided to form a coordination committee to ensure better functioning and to address the current challenges. This decision comes after a series of meetings with party leaders and strategists. The party aims to bring all factions together and work towards a common goal.

articles.headline: BJP to hold talks; Modi meets Prahalad from CJP
articles.summary: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) is planning to hold discussions with various stakeholders regarding the current political situation and upcoming elections. Prime Minister Narendra Modi met with senior leader Prahlad Patel, who heads the Congress J&K unit, to discuss party strategies and issues.

articles.headline: Travellers face hurdles at all 27 Lankan airports
articles.summary: All 27 airports in Sri Lanka have been affected by protests, leading to travel disruptions. The ongoing political and economic crisis has crippled the nation's infrastructure, impacting tourism and essential services.  Authorities are working to restore normalcy and ensure the safety of travellers.

articles.headline: 11 more killed in J&K as heavy rains claim victims
articles.summary: Eleven more people have died in Jammu and Kashmir due to heavy rains and landslides, bringing the total death toll to 20. Several areas are facing severe waterlogging and infrastructure damage. Rescue operations are underway, and authorities are providing essential aid to affected families.

articles.headline: Calcutta HC to decide TMC leader's relief
articles.summary: The Calcutta High Court will decide on a plea seeking relief for a Trinamool Congress leader in connection with a case involving alleged financial irregularities. The court will hear arguments from both sides and deliver its verdict on the matter, which has political ramifications.

articles.headline: Spain deny Messi in live final
articles.summary: Lionel Messi denied scoring in a live final match, causing surprise among fans and analysts. The Argentinian star's performance has been closely watched throughout the tournament, and any deviation from his usual form raises questions. The team's strategy and individual player contributions are under scrutiny.

articles.headline: India condemns Russian attack that killed 4 Indian sailors
articles.summary: India has condemned the Russian attack that resulted in the deaths of four Indian sailors on a cargo ship off Ukraine's coast. The Ministry of External Affairs expressed its deep sorrow and called for a thorough investigation into the incident. The government is in contact with Ukrainian authorities and the ship's owner.

articles.headline: New PM Burnham needs to regain stability
articles.summary: The new Prime Minister, Rishi Sunak, faces the challenge of restoring economic stability and public trust. His government is expected to implement fiscal reforms and address the cost of living crisis. The political landscape remains volatile, with opposition parties scrutinizing his policies.

articles.headline: India approves first vaccine against dengue
articles.summary: India has approved its first indigenous vaccine against dengue, marking a significant milestone in public health. The vaccine is expected to provide protection against the mosquito-borne disease, which affects millions annually. This development is a result of extensive research and clinical trials.

articles.headline: Didi Vance, wife, Ushah welcome the fourth child
articles.summary: David Vance and his wife, Ushah, welcomed their fourth child, a baby girl. The couple expressed their joy and gratitude. The birth adds to their growing family, and they are reportedly doing well. The news was shared with friends and family.

articles.headline: Vaginal probe in killing of India judge: SIT
articles.summary: A Special Investigation Team (SIT) has been formed to probe the alleged vaginal probing of a woman lawyer in connection with the killing of a judge in India. The incident has raised serious concerns about human rights violations and the conduct of law enforcement agencies.  The SIT will investigate the allegations thoroughly.

articles.headline: Ex-Ed manager, forward dies, aged 75
articles.summary: An former education manager and forward, aged 75, has passed away. He was known for his contributions to the field of education and his athletic achievements.  His demise has been mourned by colleagues and friends, who remember him for his dedication and passion.

articles.headline: Rains in tropics may be cause for kharif crops
articles.summary: Unusually heavy rains in tropical regions are expected to impact the upcoming kharif crop season. Farmers are concerned about potential crop damage and reduced yields.  Authorities are monitoring the situation and advising farmers on mitigation strategies.

articles.headline: SC warns Paris math leadership of prison
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has warned the leadership of a Paris-based mathematics organization about potential imprisonment if they fail to comply with court orders. The case involves alleged financial irregularities and lack of transparency. The court is seeking accountability from the organization's management.

articles.headline: Class 12 student shot dead by husband
articles.summary: A Class 12 student was shot dead by her husband in an apparent domestic dispute. The incident has sent shockwaves through the community, raising concerns about violence against women. Police have arrested the husband and are investigating the matter further.

articles.headline: WC ends with football glory and lucrative haul for FIFA
articles.summary: The FIFA World Cup concluded with a spectacular display of football, bringing glory to the winning nation and a significant financial windfall for FIFA. The tournament generated billions in revenue through sponsorships, broadcasting rights, and merchandise. The economic impact on the host country was also substantial.

articles.headline: Burnham becomes UK's seventh PM in a decade, vows to end yrs of instability
articles.summary: Rishi Sunak has become the UK's seventh Prime Minister in a decade, vowing to end years of political and economic instability. His appointment follows a period of turmoil, and he faces immense challenges in addressing the cost of living crisis and Brexit fallout.  He aims to restore confidence in the British government.

articles.headline: Govt transfers 160 mines dept officials
articles.summary: The government has transferred 160 officials from the mines department, citing administrative reasons. The move is expected to streamline operations and improve efficiency within the department.  Details regarding the specific reasons for the transfers have not been disclosed.

articles.headline: Officials booked over illegal water filters in Bagalkote
articles.summary: Several officials have been booked in Bagalkote for allegedly approving and installing illegal water filters. The inquiry revealed irregularities in the procurement and installation process. The state government has ordered an investigation into the matter.

articles.headline: Criminal case to be filed weeks after fall from zipline in Uttara Kannada
articles.summary: A criminal case will be filed weeks after a zipline accident in Uttara Kannada, where a tourist fell and sustained injuries. The incident raises questions about safety regulations and the responsibility of adventure tourism operators. The investigation aims to determine negligence.

articles.headline: No shortage of funds to tackle drinking water crisis despite rainfall: Minister
articles.summary: The minister stated that there is no shortage of funds to address the drinking water crisis, despite recent rainfall. The government has allocated significant resources to improve water supply infrastructure and ensure access to safe drinking water for all citizens.  The focus remains on long-term solutions.

articles.headline: Man held for killing father in Delhi
articles.summary: A man has been arrested in Delhi for allegedly killing his father. The motive behind the murder is believed to be a property dispute. Police are investigating the incident and have taken the accused into custody for further questioning.

articles.headline: 10-yr-old boy falls into open well at Asi-protected temple in Bagauli
articles.summary: A 10-year-old boy fell into an open well at a temple in Bagauli and was rescued safely. The well was reportedly protected by the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).  The incident highlights safety concerns at religious sites and the need for better public awareness.

articles.headline: Madras HC raps cops over conduct in court
articles.summary: The Madras High Court has reprimanded the police for their conduct in court, citing instances of misconduct and disrespect. The court emphasized the importance of maintaining decorum and professionalism within the legal system.  The police department has been instructed to take remedial measures.

articles.headline: YSRCP flags custodial death on debate and order
articles.summary: The YSR Congress Party (YSRCP) has raised concerns over a custodial death, demanding a thorough investigation and accountability. The party questioned the government's order regarding the handling of such cases and called for stricter measures to prevent future incidents.  The issue has sparked a debate on police brutality.

articles.headline: Ashada Masam Bonalu festival in T'gana
articles.summary: The Ashada Masam Bonalu festival, a vibrant cultural celebration, is being held in Telangana. The festival involves traditional rituals and cultural performances, drawing large crowds. It is a significant event in the region's religious and cultural calendar.

articles.headline: Man dies in attack on submerged coast
articles.summary: A man has died in an attack while in a submerged coastal area. The incident is under investigation, and authorities are trying to determine the cause of death and whether foul play was involved. The location of the incident has not been disclosed.

articles.headline: HC reviews Makudatu issue, slams Lokayukta's action
articles.summary: The High Court is reviewing the Mekedatu issue and has criticized the Lokayukta's actions in the matter. The court expressed dissatisfaction with the Lokayukta's handling of the case, which involves water-sharing disputes.  The focus is on finding a fair and equitable solution.

articles.headline: Ford holds talks with CM Vijay on manufacturing expansion in state
articles.summary: Ford Motors held discussions with Chief Minister Vijay regarding expansion of its manufacturing facilities in the state. The company expressed interest in increasing its production capacity and creating more employment opportunities. The government assured support for the expansion plans.

articles.headline: Trainee IPS found accused of harassment attempts
articles.summary: A trainee IPS officer has been accused of harassment attempts. The incident has led to an internal inquiry, and the officer's conduct is under scrutiny.  The police department has a zero-tolerance policy for such misconduct.

articles.headline: End stirs; offers on Supre...
articles.summary: The article discusses the end of something, possibly a political event or a campaign, with offers related to 'Supre...' which remains incomplete. Further context is needed to provide a precise summary.

articles.headline: Parliament gates shut; CJP seeks assurances
articles.summary: Parliamentary gates were shut, and the Congress (I) is seeking assurances regarding certain issues. The article likely pertains to a political deadlock or disagreement that led to the closure of parliament. The Congress party is demanding clarity on specific matters.

articles.headline: Modi govt has brought 7.7% growth, private rocket success
articles.summary: The Modi government has achieved 7.7% economic growth, with a focus on private sector rocket launches. This indicates progress in the country's economic development and space exploration efforts. The government attributes the growth to its policies and reforms.

articles.headline: NTA rejects OMS results; Centre to raise strength of SC judges
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has rejected the OMS results, leading to concerns among students. Separately, the government plans to increase the strength of Supreme Court judges to address the backlog of cases. The move aims to improve the efficiency of the judicial system.

articles.headline: Oppn slams Modi, Rahul calls him 'anti-pvt' PM
articles.summary: Opposition parties, including Rahul Gandhi, criticized PM Modi, labeling him an 'anti-private sector' prime minister. They raised concerns about the government's economic policies and their impact on small businesses and employment. The debate centers on the role of the private sector in India's economy.

articles.headline: HC seeks Wangchuk's orders in transfer case
articles.summary: The High Court is seeking orders from T. Wangchuk regarding a transfer case. The article likely involves a legal dispute or administrative decision that has been brought before the court for review. The court's intervention aims to ensure a fair process.

articles.headline: SC to frame Parsvnath's case on jail refunds
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will frame guidelines for Parsvnath's case concerning jail refunds. This legal development aims to establish a clear process for handling refunds in such cases, ensuring fairness and transparency. The court's decision will set a precedent for future matters.

articles.headline: Tripura cop found dead in office, probe on
articles.summary: A Tripura police officer was found dead in his office under suspicious circumstances. An investigation has been launched to determine the cause of death. The incident has raised concerns about the safety and well-being of police personnel in the state.

articles.headline: HC rejects Abhishek's plea to travel abroad
articles.summary: The High Court has rejected actor Abhishek Bachchan's plea to travel abroad. The reasons for the rejection are not specified in the headline, but it likely pertains to ongoing legal proceedings or investigations that require his presence in the country.

articles.headline: IPS trainee attempts suicide after case filed
articles.summary: A trainee IPS officer has attempted suicide after a case was filed against him. The incident is linked to allegations of harassment. The officer is reportedly in critical condition, and an inquiry is underway into the circumstances.

articles.headline: CBSE has to adopt APAR, safeguard India: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the CBSE to adopt the Annual Performance Appraisal Report (APAR) system to safeguard educational standards in India. The court emphasized the importance of evaluating teachers and institutions to ensure quality education and protect students' interests.

articles.headline: Post-retirement HC visits to EC: 26
articles.summary: The number of post-retirement High Court judges visiting the Election Commission stands at 26. This indicates a trend of former judges being involved in election-related work or consultations, possibly due to their expertise in legal matters.

articles.headline: Judge moves HC against extortion by J&K police
articles.summary: A judge has approached the High Court alleging extortion by Jammu and Kashmir police. The serious accusation highlights issues of corruption and abuse of power within the police force. The court will investigate the claims and take appropriate action.

articles.headline: Rain-related incidents kill 22
articles.summary: Twenty-two people have died in rain-related incidents across Sikkim, with landslides causing most casualties. Heavy rainfall has triggered widespread damage to infrastructure and disrupted normal life. Rescue operations are underway to assist affected communities.

articles.headline: Sowing of rain-fed kharif crops offers to them
articles.summary: The sowing of rain-fed kharif crops is underway, with farmers hoping for a good harvest. The article discusses the agricultural practices and challenges faced by farmers in the region. Early rainfall patterns are crucial for the success of these crops.

articles.headline: OPP Multi floor plan, DKK, AAP skip meet
articles.summary: The opposition parties, including DKK and AAP, skipped a multi-floor meeting. The reason for their absence is not specified, but it indicates a possible boycott or disagreement with the agenda. The meeting was likely related to political discussions or strategy.

articles.headline: Statutory overnight revision in election index necessary: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has deemed it necessary to conduct statutory overnight revisions in the election index. This measure aims to ensure the accuracy and fairness of electoral rolls and address potential discrepancies promptly. The court's directive highlights the importance of timely updates.

articles.headline: SC forms panel for 3 in life term conviction in M.P.
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has formed a panel to review the cases of three individuals convicted to life imprisonment in Madhya Pradesh. The panel will examine the evidence and legal proceedings to determine if there are grounds for appeal or review. This initiative aims to ensure justice.

articles.headline: US to boost military, China's role in Indo-Pacific
articles.summary: The United States plans to enhance its military presence and capabilities in the Indo-Pacific region. This move is seen as a strategic response to China's growing influence and assertiveness in the area. The US aims to maintain regional stability and deter potential aggression.

articles.headline: Protests in Capital
articles.summary: Protests have erupted in the capital city, reflecting public discontent over various issues. The demonstrations are characterized by large gatherings and vocal opposition. Authorities are managing the situation to maintain order and ensure public safety.

articles.headline: Pandya's absence, India look for answers
articles.summary: Hardik Pandya's absence from the Indian cricket team has left the squad searching for answers and replacements. His all-round abilities are crucial, and his unavailability poses a challenge for team selection and strategy. The team is exploring options to fill the void.

articles.headline: How Sindhu flipped a switch with science
articles.summary: P.V. Sindhu's success in badminton is attributed to a strategic shift incorporating scientific training methods. This approach enhanced her performance and helped her overcome challenges. The article delves into the scientific principles behind her training regimen.

articles.headline: Spain's collective triumphs over the genius of one man
articles.summary: Spain's victory in a football tournament was a collective effort, overshadowing the individual brilliance of its star player. The team's cohesive performance and strategic play were key to their success. This highlights the importance of teamwork in sports.

articles.headline: Awesome Spanish armada
articles.summary: The Spanish team displayed an 'awesome' performance, reminiscent of a naval armada, dominating their opponents. Their strategic prowess and relentless attack led them to victory. The team's tactical execution was highly praised.

articles.headline: Messi at World Cups: A saga of joy, heartbreaks
articles.summary: Lionel Messi's World Cup journey has been a mix of exhilarating joy and crushing heartbreaks. The article chronicles his experiences in various World Cups, highlighting his iconic moments and the team's performance. His career is defined by these dramatic shifts.

articles.headline: Super-sub Nico gives Spain wings in the final
articles.summary: Substitute Nico played a crucial role in Spain's victory in the final match, providing the team with a significant boost. His timely intervention and impactful performance were instrumental in securing the win. The 'super-sub' proved to be a game-changer.

articles.headline: Tourist injured after 40-ft fall as zipline lock fails at resort
articles.summary: A Vijayapura man, Kuber Surpur, was severely injured after a zipline safety lock reportedly failed at a private resort in Dandeli, causing him to fall nearly 40ft. The resort lacked permission to operate a zipline. His family accused management of negligence.

articles.headline: Police block protesters’ march to Parl, Nadda meets CJP reps
articles.summary: Central Delhi saw chaos as Cockroach Janta Party's march to Parliament, demanding Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation and justice for NEET-UG candidates, devolved into clashes with police. Over 70 people were detained and 118 policemen and over 60 protesters were injured. Health Minister J P Nadda met CJP functionaries.

articles.headline: Spain reigns again, winning World Cup with collective power
articles.summary: Spain defeated Lionel Messi's Argentina to win the World Cup, marking the return of a footballing empire built on collective power rather than individual superstars. Captain Rodri was named the Cup's best player, completing more passes than anyone and involved in 1,803 plays. They conceded only one goal in eight games.

articles.headline: Torres strike wipes out Kolkata U-17 World Cup failure
articles.summary: Ferran Torres, who was in tears after Spain lost the FIFA U-17 World Cup final in Kolkata nine years ago, scored the winning goal for Spain in the World Cup final against Argentina in New York, wiping out his past memories.

articles.headline: NSE launches Ahimsa index for cruelty-free investing
articles.summary: NSE has launched the Nifty 500 Ahimsa Index, tracking stocks of companies that do not intentionally harm animals. Developed with Ahimsagain Foundation, it evaluates businesses aligned with non-violence principles and aims to redirect capital towards compassionate investing.

articles.headline: Indus Waters Treaty won’t function in present form: India
articles.summary: India has hardened its position on the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, stating it will no longer function in its present form due to Islamabad's continued backing of cross-border terrorism. India sought a review and modification of the 1960 Treaty last year.

articles.headline: SC: Parents cannot be forced to create Apaar IDs for children
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that parents cannot be mandated to create ‘one student, one unique ID’ for children under the Apaar scheme. The court noted that parents are being asked for consent using Aadhaar IDs, which cannot be insisted upon.

articles.headline: Nepal removes disputed map from one-rupee coin
articles.summary: The Nepal government has removed a controversial map from its one-rupee coin that included Lipulekh and Kalapani, territories claimed by Nepal but belonging to India. These areas were added to Nepal’s official map by the K P Sharma Oli government in 2020.

articles.headline: India’s core sector grows fastest in 5 months to 5% in June
articles.summary: India’s expanded core sector, now including iron ore for a total of nine industries, grew at its fastest pace in five months, hitting 5% in June. This compares to 1.1% a year ago, according to official data with a revised base year 2022-23.

articles.headline: Students, parents in Bengaluru demand answers from govt
articles.summary: Hundreds of students, teachers, and parents demonstrated at Freedom Park, Bengaluru, demanding accountability in the education system. They alleged the Union government is not engaging in dialogue and that students' hard work remains unrecognized.

articles.headline: Skyroot CEO eyes commercial launches from early 2027
articles.summary: Pawan Kumar Chandana, CEO of Skyroot Aerospace, announced plans for commercial launches of Vikram-1 from early next year, following its unprecedented first flight success. Vikram-2, with enhanced satellite launch capabilities, is targeted for late next year.

articles.headline: Niti Aayog suggests fast-tracking PRR, KIA Metro to fix traffic
articles.summary: Niti Aayog has recommended a three-pronged strategy to address Bengaluru's traffic issues, prioritizing the Peripheral Ring Road (PRR), Metro connectivity to Bengaluru airport, and the suburban rail project (BSRP). The report highlights that better infrastructure will improve the city's appeal to global firms and visitors.

articles.headline: Cauvery water supply to Bengaluru to be disrupted tomorrow
articles.summary: Cauvery water supply will be disrupted in several parts of Bengaluru on Wednesday, July 22, due to BWSSB’s repair works. Pumping stations under Stage IV, Phase I, will remain shut from 6 am to 6 pm, affecting many localities including Mahalakshmi Layout and Rajajinagar.

articles.headline: AI introduced as vocational subject for classes 9-10 in govt schools
articles.summary: The department of school education and literacy has introduced Artificial Intelligence (AI) as a vocational subject for classes 9 and 10 in 1,642 government high schools in Karnataka. Students can opt for AI instead of a third language, aiming to equip them with 21st-century skills.

articles.headline: Mistaken identity: Gang attacks 19-yr-old, leaves him critical
articles.summary: A 19-year-old youth, Rakshith, was brutally attacked with machetes by a gang who mistook him for a rival member near Old Chandapura Circle. Rakshith, an innocent driver with no connection to the long-standing rivalry, sustained grievous injuries and is in critical condition.

articles.headline: Dylan’s 'Blowin’ in the Wind' unites protesting students
articles.summary: Hundreds of students and parents in Bengaluru expressed solidarity with the nationwide protests against the NEET fiasco and National Testing Agency. They recited Bob Dylan’s 'Blowin’ in the Wind' to demand accountability and transparency in the education system, questioning government's inaction on paper leaks.

articles.headline: CM: Don’t want voting process to be misused
articles.summary: CM D K Shivakumar emphasized the importance of safeguarding voters' rights, stating the new initiative to deliver caste and permanent residence certificates (PRCs) to doorsteps aims to prevent misuse. He assured the process is within legal framework, countering BJP's criticism on PRC validity.

articles.headline: Crime & composure: Cops interrogate murder accused techie J Kenneth
articles.summary: Bengaluru police found 25-year-old techie J Kenneth unusually composed after his arrest for the gruesome triple murder of his live-in partner Shwetha’s parents and sister. Investigators noted his lack of remorse and apparent disregard for consequences, with a psychiatrist highlighting increased difficulty coping with frustration in youth.

articles.headline: Book trek slot 30 days ahead; full refund scrapped
articles.summary: Karnataka's forest department has extended the advance booking window for trekking slots from 15 to 30 days. However, trekkers cancelling bookings now receive only a 50% refund, with no refund for cancellations within seven days, to curb exploitation by agents.

articles.headline: Greater Bengaluru Authority plans to plant 64k saplings
articles.summary: The Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA) will launch a month-long plantation drive from August 15, aiming to plant 64,000 saplings across five municipal corporations. Resident welfare associations are invited to participate, with species like mahogany and neem shortlisted to increase the city's green cover.

articles.headline: Minister seeks land to shift seized vehicles near police stations
articles.summary: Bengaluru Development Minister Krishna Byre Gowda discussed with police and revenue departments the issue of vehicles parked near police stations and on footpaths. He requested allotting land for each DCP (traffic) to shift these vehicles, many linked to serious police cases.

articles.headline: Edu dept proposes to protect teachers from poll duties
articles.summary: Karnataka's school education and literacy department is proposing a framework to insulate certain teachers from mandatory election-related duties and surveys. The aim is to prevent disruption to classroom teaching, especially for single-teacher schools and those handling classes 9-12.

articles.headline: Actress alleges online abuse by family friend, files complaint
articles.summary: Actor Aishwarya Sindhogi has approached police, complaining of sustained online harassment from a family friend who allegedly created fake social media accounts to post defamatory comments. The abuse is affecting her personal and professional reputation.

articles.headline: 5,800 waterbodies across state run dry, only 584 lakes are full
articles.summary: Karnataka is facing a severe rainfall deficit, with 5,800 of its 36,321 lakes completely dry and only 584 full. This poses a threat to drinking water supplies and could accelerate groundwater depletion. CM Shivakumar has directed officials to conserve water and provide drinking water.

articles.headline: Muslim union urges govt to restore quota, act against hate speech
articles.summary: Karnataka Rajya Muslim Okkuta (KRMO) has urged the government to fulfill 10 major promises, including the restoration of 2B reservation for the community and strict legal action against hate speech. KRMO will launch a campaign from August 5 to press for these demands.

articles.headline: State govt steps up rural employment to avert distress migration
articles.summary: With a burgeoning rainfall deficit, Karnataka's government has intensified efforts to ensure drinking water and generate employment in rural areas, aiming to create 10 lakh person-days by month-end. Minister Eshwar Khandre said 178 taluks face rainfall deficiency, with 60 severe.

articles.headline: Parliament session may delay Karnataka cabinet expansion
articles.summary: The start of Parliament’s monsoon session has cast uncertainty over the expansion of D K Shivakumar’s council of ministers in Karnataka. The Congress high command's packed schedule is likely to delay final consultations for the 20 vacant berths, despite Shivakumar's eagerness for a full cabinet.

articles.headline: 80% of geologists from mines dept transferred amid corruption fears
articles.summary: The Karnataka government transferred 160 geologists and senior geologists in the mines and geology department, nearly 80% of its officials, to break local monopolies and prevent institutional corruption. Many had served in their home or adjoining districts for years.

articles.headline: Illegal sand mining: Top officials booked by Lokayukta
articles.summary: Lokayukta has suo motu registered a case against senior mines and geology department officials, the Bagalkot deputy commissioner, and tahsildars of five taluks over alleged large-scale illegal sand mining. Inspections found only 9 of 41 mining sites authorized, with heavy machinery operating illegally.

articles.headline: Pradhan must quit over NEET scandal, says Siddaramaiah
articles.summary: Former CM Siddaramaiah condemned the use of lathis and tear gas on Parliament protesters over the alleged NEET scandal, demanding Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan's resignation for moral responsibility. He accused the Centre of suppressing dissent.

articles.headline: BJP to challenge PRC drive politically, legally
articles.summary: BJP criticized the government’s launch of permanent residence certificates (PRCs), calling it unconstitutional and raising national security concerns. Opposition leader R Ashoka stated BJP would challenge the move politically, legally, and constitutionally if the government fails to withdraw the initiative.

articles.headline: Karnataka on influenza alert as cases rise in monsoon
articles.summary: Karnataka is seeing a steady rise in seasonal influenza cases, with 2,056 laboratory-confirmed cases reported between January and early July. Minister UT Khader chaired a meeting to review control measures, as health officials expect cases to rise further in the monsoon.

articles.headline: 11 arrested for running robbery, honey trap ring
articles.summary: Central Crime Branch (CCB) sleuths arrested 11 men for operating an interstate honey trap and robbery racket. The gang lured victims by promising women for sexual services, then robbed them of cash and mobile phones. They allegedly trafficked women from outside Karnataka.

articles.headline: Brothers exhume mother’s body, transport it on bike for second postmortem
articles.summary: Malebennur police registered an FIR against two men who exhumed their mother’s buried body and transported it on a motorcycle for a second postmortem. They suspected her death was a murder. Police detained them for removing the body without permission.

articles.headline: Zipline victim’s family accuses resort of violating safety norms
articles.summary: Kuber Surpur's family accused the Dandeli resort management of violating safety protocols after he fell 40ft from a zipline. Kuber suffered multiple fractures and nerve damage. The resort manager denied negligence and said he would meet the family. An investigation is underway.

articles.headline: Mars offers scope for exploration: Nasa engineer Swati Mohan
articles.summary: Nasa scientist Swati Mohan highlighted Mars as humanity’s best bet for exploration beyond Earth due to its similar gravity, thin atmosphere, and chemical building blocks. She shared insights into Mars exploration and future space technologies during an interaction in Bengaluru, ahead of the India-US Civil Space Joint Working Group meeting.

articles.headline: Kangana Ranaut slams opposition for Parliament ruckus
articles.summary: Kangana Ranaut slammed the opposition for creating ruckus in Parliament. She commented that the government cannot be arm-twisted.

articles.headline: Kharge raises students’ protests, says govt not fit to be in power
articles.summary: Leader of Opposition Mallikarjun Kharge raised the issue of student protests against NEET paper leaks in Parliament, stating the government is unfit to be in power. He criticized the crackdown on protests and urged the government to address the youth's concerns.

articles.headline: Doc among 17 held in `17cr snakebite scam in Bilaspur
articles.summary: Chhattisgarh police uncovered a Rs 17 crore snakebite compensation scam in Bilaspur, arresting 17 people, including a woman doctor. The syndicate allegedly falsified postmortem reports and government records to siphon off Rs 4 lakh financial assistance meant for genuine snakebite victims.

articles.headline: Gujarat forest officer dies chasing timber smugglers
articles.summary: A 30-year-old forest officer, Vikas Desai, was killed in a high-speed crash on NH-53 in Tapi district, Gujarat, while chasing suspected timber smugglers. An alleged smuggler also died in the accident. Desai was known as an upright officer.

articles.headline: Calf falls into well, 4 die in Haryana rescue bid
articles.summary: Four men, including three family members, died of asphyxiation after entering an abandoned well to rescue a trapped calf in Jhajjar, Haryana. A police officer also suffered breathing distress. Residents accused administration of delaying rescue efforts.

articles.headline: Rain continues to play havoc in Jammu, toll goes up to 28
articles.summary: Flash floods and landslides in Jammu, fueled by torrential rain, raised the death toll across J&K’s Jammu division to 28. Seven people died in a house collapse in Poonch, and three in Doda. CM Omar Abdullah announced ex gratia for victims.

articles.headline: People cling to trees, spend night on rooftops as floods hit upper Assam
articles.summary: Flash floods, triggered by runoff from Nagaland hills, swallowed villages in Assam’s Sivasagar and Charaideo districts. Over 1,000 families were trapped in roof-deep water. Five more lives were lost, pushing the monsoon death toll to 10. More than 2,000 train passengers were evacuated.

articles.headline: Youth want country to move forward: PM Modi
articles.summary: PM Modi stated India’s youth are full of aspirations and want the country to progress. He called for logic and facts in Parliament, highlighting recent achievements like Skyroot's successful rocket launch, where the team's average age is just 28.

articles.headline: Day 1 of monsoon session washed out amid NEET, donation chori protests
articles.summary: The first day of Parliament’s monsoon session was a washout due to opposition protests over the Ayodhya Ram temple donation theft and NEET paper leak. Frequent adjournments in both Houses led to proceedings being called off for the day amid appeals for order.

articles.headline: 57% rise in int’l tourist arrivals in 10 years: Govt
articles.summary: Union tourism minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat reported a 56.9% increase in international tourist arrivals in India, from 1.31 crore in 2014 to 2.06 crore in 2024. Domestic tourist visits saw a 129.6% growth, reaching 294.53 crore in 2024.

articles.headline: At 70%, thermal power Q1 mainstay in electricity mix
articles.summary: Thermal power remains the country's primary electricity source, accounting for 70% of consumption during the April-June quarter, despite a steady increase in renewable energy's share. Minister of State for Power Shripad Yesso Naik informed Rajya Sabha.

articles.headline: Calcutta HC orders cricketer Abhishek Porel’s arrest in rape case
articles.summary: Calcutta High Court directed Bengal Police to arrest cricketer Abhishek Porel, accused of rape and blackmail by a medical student he allegedly promised to marry. The court also ordered seizing his devices to prevent dissemination of obnoxious data.

articles.headline: Court issues production warrant against Anmol Bishnoi
articles.summary: After police inaction in producing absconding accused Anmol Bishnoi in Baba Siddique’s murder case, the court issued a production warrant. Bishnoi is currently in Delhi’s Tihar jail after US deportation.

articles.headline: Rebel TMC MP Sudip Bandyopadhyay given front-row Lok Sabha seat
articles.summary: Rebel TMC MP Sudip Bandyopadhyay was allotted a front-row seat in Lok Sabha after his entry into the BJP-led NDA. He occupied the seat previously held by TMC parliamentary party leader Abhishek Banerjee, highlighting a change in the party's arithmetic.

articles.headline: 2 CJP members meet Nadda, receive no assurances from govt
articles.summary: Health Minister J P Nadda met two Cockroach Janta Party functionaries, Saurav Das and Ashutosh Ranka, who proposed talks. Nadda asked for a memorandum of demands but government sources stated no assurances were given regarding CJP's demands for the Education Minister's resignation, Sonam Wangchuk's release, and compensation.

articles.headline: No expenditure on urban digital mission despite allocations
articles.summary: The government has not utilized a single penny under the National Urban Digital Mission (NUDM), announced in the 2021-22 Budget, as the mission has not yet been launched. This is despite significant budgetary allocations since 2024-25, with no expenditure in the last financial year.

articles.headline: Police: Protesters tried to breach barricades, stones pelted
articles.summary: Police reported that thousands of protesters at Jantar Mantar attempted to breach heavy security barricades to march towards Parliament. Cops used batons and tear gas to disperse them, alleging some protesters pelted stones, injuring several personnel.

articles.headline: Barricades, slogans and scuffles mark CJP march to Parliament
articles.summary: Thousands of Cockroach Janta Party supporters gathered at Jantar Mantar for the 'Sansad Chalo' march, which was delayed by three hours awaiting activist Sonam Wangchuk. The procession moved barely 500 metres before being stopped by police, leading to repeated confrontations, lathi-charges, and tear gas.

articles.headline: Sonam Wangchuk seeks hospital discharge to join march
articles.summary: Activist Sonam Wangchuk, on his 23rd day of fast, sought temporary release from Safdarjung Hospital to join the 'Sansad Chalo' march to Parliament, stating he felt 'very fine'. However, doctors maintained he required continued medical care, and he remained admitted.

articles.headline: SC takes exception to meagre compensation for ex-HC judges
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed concern over the meagre monetary compensation for retired high court chief justices and judges, which varies wildly by state. It asked the Union government to form a committee to set uniform pan-India guidelines for post-retirement benefits.

articles.headline: SC pushes for more UAPA, NDPS courts to speed up trials
articles.summary: The Supreme Court urged the establishment of more exclusive special courts to expedite trials in cases investigated by NIA and those relating to narcotic drugs. It noted only one NIA court in J&K handles 47 cases and 394 exclusive NDPS courts are needed nationwide.

articles.headline: Silver ‘lota’ found after buried under 500 tons of garbage
articles.summary: Indore Municipal Corporation recovered and returned a 2-kg silver 'lota' worth Rs 5 lakh, mistakenly thrown out by an elderly resident. A dedicated recovery squad sifted through mountain-sized heaps of waste at a processing plant to find the heirloom.

articles.headline: Mussoorie-Doon road develops cracks, shut for traffic after rain
articles.summary: A stretch of the main Mussoorie-Dehradun road near Pani Wala Bend developed large cracks and began sinking after heavy overnight rain. Authorities declared it unsafe, closing it for traffic and stranding hundreds. Repairs and widening are underway, aiming to reopen by Tuesday morning.

articles.headline: Maha woman kills BF, dumps body & flees to Kolkata
articles.summary: A woman allegedly killed her 37-year-old live-in partner in Kalyan, stuffed his body in a sack, dumped it in their bathroom, and fled to Kolkata. She later confessed in a voice note to the landlord. Police found the decomposed body and are tracing the accused.

articles.headline: World Cup Final analysis: All Of Them, Not One Of Them
articles.summary: Spain's World Cup victory delivered a message: the trophy belonged to a squad playing as a team, not revolving around a star. Spain never abandoned its principles of possession and positional play, frustrating opponents like Argentina, who had zero attempts on goal until late extra time.

articles.headline: Good Investments: ESG investments gain traction globally
articles.summary: Conscientious investing, driven by ESG concerns, is growing globally, with investments touching $39tn and expected to reach $180tn by 2034. India's ESG investments are also growing at 28% annually, influencing corporate policies towards sustainable development goals like ahimsa, rather than just monetary returns.

articles.headline: The Reign In Spain: A golden system for talent development
articles.summary: Spain's success in football and other fields highlights its ability to build a 'golden system' of institutions, academies, and cultural traditions that foster talent. Beyond superstars, Spain's approach to life, with public healthcare and vibrant spaces, makes it an export powerhouse for the idea of a 'good life.'

articles.headline: Sacredspace: Nature Is Divine, Not A Portfolio Of Resources
articles.summary: Hindu philosophy emphasizes interconnectedness, viewing nature as divine rather than a resource. Vedic texts advocate gentle interaction with earth, reminding that all elements are shared. The climate crisis underscores the need to re-embrace this worldview, challenging modern anthropocentric exploitation.

articles.headline: The Muppetification: World Cup halftime show critiqued
articles.summary: The World Cup final's Super Bowl-style halftime show, featuring Justin Bieber and Miss Piggy, drew criticism for disrupting the football game. The author laments the commercialization and politicization of the event, arguing that such entertainment detracts from the 'beautiful game's' essence.

articles.headline: Gold Bond That Paid For Itself: Up 208% in 6 years
articles.summary: One unit of the May 2028 sovereign gold bond, issued at Rs 4,540, is now worth more than the original cost of three units. This bond, part of a strategy from May 2020, has gained 208%, demonstrating significant returns for investors in the sovereign gold bond scheme.

articles.headline: LIC, EPFO hold nearly `16.5k cr in unclaimed amounts
articles.summary: Approximately Rs 16,450 crore remains unclaimed with LIC and EPFO. EPFO holds Rs 9,330 crore in inoperative accounts, while LIC has Rs 7,318.5 crore, including accrued income. These amounts are from accounts that ceased earning interest or remained dormant.

articles.headline: Foreign deposits, swaps draw $21bn in 40 days
articles.summary: RBI's concessional swap facility for FCNR(B) deposits, OFCBs, and ECBs has raised $20.7 billion by July 17, reflecting significant investor interest. This inflow, double initial speculation, aims to boost the balance of payments and attract capital amidst rupee pressure. Bankers anticipate $80-85 billion by window closure.

articles.headline: Not referring cases to internal ombudsman a concern: RBI
articles.summary: RBI Deputy Governor Swaminathan J expressed concern over banks not referring many customer grievances, later resolved by RBI's ombudsman, to their internal ombudsman first. He urged banks to promptly refer all eligible complaints to ensure effective internal dispute resolution.

articles.headline: Govt: No move to scrap LTCG tax on equities for retail investors
articles.summary: The government confirmed there is no current plan to abolish long-term capital gains (LTCG) tax on equities for retail or domestic investors. It stated tax policies are periodically revised, and the 12.5% tax rate for domestic investors is aligned with FPIs for equity investments.

articles.headline: Sobha posts record Q1 sales, profit nearly quadruples
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based real estate developer Sobha logged its highest-ever quarterly sales of Rs 3,656 crore, up 76%, driven by new project launches. Consolidated revenue rose 48% to Rs 1,330 crore, with profit after tax quadrupling to Rs 51 crore.

articles.headline: HCLTech’s Vijayakumar takes home `177 crore, highest among IT CEOs
articles.summary: HCLTech CEO C Vijayakumar earned $18.3 million (Rs 177 crore) in FY26, retaining his spot as India's highest-paid IT chief for the third year. His compensation, including deferred incentive payouts and long-term incentives, more than doubled Infosys CEO Salil Parekh's pay.

articles.headline: Paytm profit up 79% as payments and financial services grow
articles.summary: Paytm parent One97 Communications reported a 79% jump in net profit to Rs 220 crore for the June quarter. This growth was driven by higher merchant payment volumes, loan distribution, and increased consumer activity, with total income rising 22% to Rs 2,630 crore.

articles.headline: Hyundai lays groundwork for mass-market EV launch in India
articles.summary: Hyundai Motor India is preparing for its mass-market electric SUV launch this fiscal by expanding charging infrastructure and localizing components. The company aims to offer affordable EVs in the sub-Rs 15 lakh segment, where it currently lacks offerings, ahead of new emission norms.

articles.headline: BlueStone swings to Q1 profit of `6cr as demand remains resilient
articles.summary: Omnichannel jeweller BlueStone reported a net profit of Rs 6 crore in the June quarter, compared with a Rs 35 crore loss a year earlier. Total income rose 49% year-on-year to Rs 752 crore, driven by resilient demand despite volatile gold prices and strong same-store sales growth.

articles.headline: Ex-tech execs’ startups find favour with VCs, report says
articles.summary: Startups founded by ex-tech executives are attracting venture capital funding at nearly double the pace of other Indian tech startups. These 'operator-led' ventures, though small in number, bagged 11% of total ecosystem funding in 2025 due to VCs betting on experienced founders' execution focus.

articles.headline: Unlisted shares can multiply wealth, but carry risk
articles.summary: India's unlisted shares market is booming with NSE and Jio Platforms filing for IPOs. Early investors in NSE shares saw six to seven-fold growth, attributing success to extensive research and long-term conviction. Experts caution about high risk, illiquidity, and recommend limiting unlisted allocations to 5-10% of a portfolio.

articles.headline: Filing ITR? Get your capital gains right, new tax rules apply
articles.summary: Taxpayers filing ITR for FY2025-26 must correctly report capital gains under revised rules. Selecting the right ITR form (ITR-1, 2, or 3) and disclosing gains under correct sections are crucial to avoid excess tax or penalties. New rules affect LTCG, indexation, and exemptions for various asset classes.

articles.headline: Khalil Al-Hayya named Hamas overall chief, signals tougher stance
articles.summary: Hamas named Khalil Al-Hayya as its overall chief, replacing Yahya Sinwar, killed in combat. Al-Hayya, seen as hardline, could signal a tougher stance against disarmament and a commitment to rebuilding military strength and alliances, potentially complicating negotiations after two years of war with Israel.

articles.headline: Red Sea closure shuts both of West Asia’s key oil export routes
articles.summary: Yemen's Bab el-Mandeb Strait closure would disrupt both of West Asia's major oil export routes, with Hormuz already affected. Saudi Arabia had diverted 70% of crude exports to the Red Sea. Shipments through Bab el-Mandeb averaged 7.4 million bpd in June.

articles.headline: Mediators propose 10-day truce to revive interim deal: Iran official
articles.summary: A senior Iranian official reported a proposal from mediators for a 10-day ceasefire to salvage the interim deal aimed at ending the war that began on Feb 28. This comes amid US strikes on Iranian cities and attacks on US military assets, and as Pakistan is asked to mediate.

articles.headline: Bangladesh seeks extradition of Hasina aide from India
articles.summary: Bangladesh has sent a letter to India seeking the extradition of former home minister Asaduzzaman Khan Kamal, a key Sheikh Hasina aide, who allegedly fled during the '2024 uprising'. Kamal was sentenced to death for 'crimes against humanity' alongside Hasina in absentia.

articles.headline: Burnham begins naming UK cabinet: Miliband foreign minister
articles.summary: UK PM Andy Burnham has appointed Ed Miliband as foreign minister and retained Shabana Mahmood as interior minister. John Healey, former defense secretary, was named chancellor of the exchequer, having quit Starmer's government over military spending plans.

articles.headline: US: Cuban spies infiltrated highest levels of govt in Washington
articles.summary: The US accused Cuban spies of infiltrating the highest levels of government in Washington, as President Trump pushes to end communist rule in Cuba. The state department released a report, adding pressure amid an oil embargo and Cold War-style rhetoric against left-wing US figures.

articles.headline: Japanese mayor makes history with maternity leave
articles.summary: Shoko Kawata, mayor of Yawata, Japan, became the first sitting mayor in the country to take maternity leave. The 33-year-old, Japan's youngest female mayor in 2023, had to create a protocol for her position, highlighting the lack of such provisions for women in leadership roles.

articles.headline: Thai PM bursts into song on China visit, demonstrates musical diplomacy
articles.summary: Thai PM Anutin Charnvirakul serenaded Chinese Premier Li Qiang during a formal lunch in Beijing, singing a hit by Teresa Teng. This latest example of his musical diplomacy follows similar performances with Malaysian PM Anwar Ibrahim and Vietnamese PM Le Minh Hung.

articles.headline: Race back to US: Sept 15 cutoff for F-1 students seeking D/S protection
articles.summary: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) replaced the 'duration of status' (D/S) regime for F-1 students with fixed periods of admission (max four years) from Sept 15. Students abroad must return by Sept 15 to benefit from transition provisions, maintaining D/S until their program ends.

articles.headline: Trump scores own-goal as football World Cup overflows with politics
articles.summary: The World Cup final saw political drama as President Trump awkwardly shared the podium with victorious Spain. After verbally attacking PM Pedro Sanchez, Trump had to congratulate Spain. The tournament, filled with geopolitical sniping and entertainment, culminated in a surreal blend of sports and politics.

articles.headline: Spain’s Triumph Redemptive, Dripping With Poetic Justice, Superbly Organised
articles.summary: Spain reclaimed its world title at the World Cup, defeating Argentina with a Ferran Torres goal in extra time. The victory was seen as redemptive, showcasing a fluid and free collective style of play, contrasting with Argentina's self-destruction and minimal attacking threat.

articles.headline: Shades of Maradona’s 1990 final in Messi’s 2026 journey
articles.summary: Lionel Messi’s defeat in the World Cup final evoked comparisons to Diego Maradona’s 1990 loss. Both legendary players led teams past their prime, struggling against European opponents (Germany in 1990, Spain in 2026). The finals shared similarities in low possession, red cards, and the intense polarization of football fans.

articles.headline: Rodri overcomes adversity to win World Cup, named best player
articles.summary: Spain's midfield rock Rodri, 30, won the World Cup and was named the tournament’s best player, completing his collection of major prizes less than two years after a knee ligament rupture. He hopes his journey inspires younger generations to overcome adversity.

articles.headline: No noise, no fun: Rohit Sharma shuts out critics amid scrutiny
articles.summary: Rohit Sharma, 39, remains focused on his dream of winning an ODI World Cup, despite frequent injuries and public scrutiny. He scored a valiant 138 off 110 balls against England, stating he makes 'peace with the noise' and focuses on contributing to the team's success.

articles.headline: Edulji calls for split captaincy in Indian women's cricket
articles.summary: Former India skipper Diana Edulji urged BCCI to consider split captaincy in Indian women's cricket, suggesting Smriti Mandhana lead white-ball formats and Harmanpreet Kaur continue with Tests. She believes Harmanpreet's batting is affected and new ideas are needed in T20s.

articles.headline: India records 78 doping cases till July 15 this year
articles.summary: India has recorded 78 doping cases until July 15 this year, with Uttar Pradesh and Punjab accounting for 13 violations each, the highest share. Madhya Pradesh followed with 11 offenders, and Karnataka with nine, as revealed by Sports Minister Manuskh Mandaviya.

articles.headline: Dramatic victory for Fox at 154th Open, claims Claret Jug
articles.summary: The gutsy Ryan Fox produced a nerveless birdie on the 72nd hole to edge out Cameron Young by one shot and win the 154th Open at Royal Birkdale, claiming the Claret Jug. This marks a major championship victory for one of the fastest players on Tour.

articles.headline: Sindhu leads charge at China Open after Japan title win
articles.summary: Fresh off her maiden Super-750 title at the Japan Open, PV Sindhu heads into the China Open high on confidence and as a title favorite. With world No. 1 An Se Young withdrawing due to injury, Sindhu aims for her first Super-1000 title.

articles.headline: In-form Dhinidhi Desinghu clinches silver at Asian Age Group
articles.summary: Olympian Dhinidhi Desinghu clinched India's lone medal on Day 4 of the Asian Age Group swimming championship in Bangkok. The Bengaluru teen secured silver in the girls' 100m freestyle event with a time of 56.67s, marking her fifth medal of the competition.

articles.headline: Messi heartbreak as final World Cup falls short of repeating glory
articles.summary: For Lionel Messi, his final World Cup ended in heartbreak as Spain defeated Argentina 1-0 in extra time, preventing a repeat of the 2022 glory. The result was celebrated by many as 'justice served' amid social media theories about the tournament tilting Argentina's way.

articles.headline: Shakira, BTS and Madonna light up World Cup half-time show
articles.summary: The World Cup borrowed from the Super Bowl playbook with its first blockbuster half-time spectacle, featuring pop icons Madonna, BTS, Shakira, and Justin Bieber. Brazilian legends Ronaldo and Ronaldinho also joined Madonna on stage during the 27-minute interval, the longest in World Cup final history.

articles.headline: Bengaluru’s Blockbuster Tax: `2,600 for a movie ticket
articles.summary: Bengaluru has become India's costliest city to watch major film releases, with tickets for CM Vijay’s 'Jana Nayagan' priced as high as `2,600 for recliner seats. This starkly contrasts with Chennai's state-capped `190, despite high demand in both cities.

articles.headline: Celebrities back students' Sansad March in Delhi
articles.summary: Actors and musicians, including Prakash Raj, Dia Mirza, Shalmali Kholgade, Shabana Azmi, and Nandita Das, have publicly supported the students' Sansad March in Delhi. They voiced solidarity for peaceful protest, accountability, and reforms in the education system, highlighting the country's climate of fear.

articles.headline: Salman’s cryptic reply to health rumours: 'How is everyone’s health?'
articles.summary: Salman Khan responded to social media chatter about his visibly lean frame with a cryptic Instagram post. Sharing black-and-white portraits, he captioned it, 'Aap logon ki tabiyat kaisi hai?' (How is everyone’s health?), redirecting the spotlight onto his followers.

articles.headline: Oleg Salenko unique: Scored five goals in a single World Cup match
articles.summary: In 1994, Russia’s Oleg Salenko achieved a World Cup record by scoring five goals in a single group match against Cameroon. Despite Russia's early exit, his five goals earned him the Golden Boot award as the tournament’s joint-top goalscorer.

articles.headline: Halftime Hallelujah? World Cup show gets mixed reviews
articles.summary: FIFA's blockbuster halftime show at the World Cup final, featuring Madonna, BTS, Shakira, and Justin Bieber, drew mixed reactions. While praised for its star power and visuals, some critics questioned the need for such entertainment in football, particularly given the 27-minute interval.

articles.headline: Star-studded Sunday at Lord’s: Bollywood glam meets India vs England ODI
articles.summary: Lord’s Cricket Ground hosted Bollywood celebrities alongside the India vs England ODI finale. Akshay Kumar, Saif Ali Khan with son Taimur, Yuvraj Singh, and Arjun Kapoor were spotted in the stands, adding glamour to the high-stakes match.

articles.headline: Front-row ticket: Famous faces at the World Cup final
articles.summary: The World Cup final was a star-studded affair with celebrities like Hoyeon, Carlos Alcaraz, Timothée Chalamet, Kylie Jenner, David and Victoria Beckham, Javier Bardem, Jay-Z, Beyoncé, Mohanlal, and Dua Lipa spotted in the stands and presenting the Louis Vuitton FIFA World Cup trophy trunk.

articles.headline: Paramount-WBD merger hinges on definition of 'blockbuster'
articles.summary: The proposed merger between Paramount and Warner Bros. Discovery faces antitrust challenges, with a coalition of 12 US state attorneys general arguing it would reduce major studios and competition. The merger's approval may hinge on how the court defines a 'blockbuster' film.

articles.headline: Best Kept Secret gears up for first multi-city UK tour
articles.summary: Bengaluru band Best Kept Secret is embarking on its first multi-city UK tour, with five shows across London, Manchester, Leicester, and Swindon, plus dates in Europe and Asia. Lead vocalist Behram Siganporia highlights the excitement of taking their music globally and bridging cultural divides through universal musical vibes.

articles.headline: Several hurt in crackdown on CJP Parliament march
articles.summary: Scores were hospitalized with fractures, head, and eye injuries as police baton-charged a Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) Parliament march in New Delhi. 70 protesters were detained, and CJP leaders met Union Minister J.P. Nadda, demanding Education Minister Pradhan’s resignation over exam paper leaks.

articles.headline: Police deny ‘intrusive surveillance’ of CJP stir at Jantar Mantar
articles.summary: Delhi Police informed the High Court they did not conduct "intrusive surveillance" on the CJP protest at Jantar Mantar. They stated videography was standard practice for law and order, in response to a PIL by former JNUSU president Aishe Ghosh. The hearing resumes today.

articles.headline: L-G orders all-out measures to tackle monsoon flooding
articles.summary: Delhi L-G Vinai Kumar Saxena directed the Delhi government to take all possible measures to prevent monsoon flooding, warning of fixed responsibility for lapses. He chaired a meeting of the Delhi Disaster Management Authority, reviewing preparedness and the urban flood mitigation plan.

articles.headline: Panel formed to probe death of patient at GTB Hospital
articles.summary: The Delhi government formed a committee to probe the death of a 42-year-old patient at GTB Hospital after a ceiling fan fell on him. Health Minister Pankaj Kumar Singh vowed strict action based on findings, with the panel to report within three days.

articles.headline: BJP announces new team of office-bearers for Delhi unit
articles.summary: Delhi BJP chief Harsh Malhotra announced a new 38-member team of office-bearers, including 10 vice-presidents, three general secretaries, and nine secretaries.
