Praveen Shankar Kapoor was reappointed media head. The team includes five appointees from Scheduled Castes and seven from OBCs.

articles.headline: DU to set up two control rooms to check ragging
articles.summary: Delhi University announced anti-ragging measures, including two control rooms, ahead of the new session. Students were cautioned against using AI to alter admission documents. 45,316 candidates confirmed admission, with the second list out July 25 and session starting July 28.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi to address city students on Aug. 9: Cong.
articles.summary: Senior Congress leader Rahul Gandhi will address a major students’ meeting in Delhi on August 9 as part of the party’s campaign against alleged paper leaks. The Congress will also hold a ‘Chhatron Ki Goonj’ event addressed by other senior leaders.

articles.headline: AAP hits out at BJP over police action on protesting students
articles.summary: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) criticized the BJP-led Central government over police action against student protesters in Delhi. AAP MP Sanjay Singh condemned the "police brutality," while Arvind Kejriwal and Atishi slammed the government's "authoritarianism" and failure to address student demands.

articles.headline: Delhi Cabinet clears Central scheme to phase out polluting commercial vehicles
articles.summary: The Delhi Cabinet approved the PARIVARTAN scheme to phase out old, polluting commercial vehicles in Delhi-NCR. Chief Minister Rekha Gupta stated the ₹9,585 crore initiative, with ₹5,041 crore from the Centre, aims to reduce air pollution by incentivizing replacement with electric and BS-VI compliant vehicles.

articles.headline: SC stays HC order granting Kalita access to probe documents
articles.summary: The Supreme Court stayed a Delhi High Court order allowing Pinjra Tod activist Devangana Kalita to inspect investigation documents not relied upon by Delhi Police in the 2020 riots ‘larger conspiracy’ case. The apex court agreed this would delay the trial.

articles.headline: Four dead after ‘inhaling toxic gas’ in Haryana well
articles.summary: Four men died in Haryana’s Jhajjar district after entering an abandoned well to rescue a calf. Police suspect inhalation of toxic gas caused their deaths. Rescue efforts were launched, but all four were declared dead at the hospital.

articles.headline: Martyrs’ Day: TMC camps, Cong. to hold separate rallies today
articles.summary: Kolkata will host three competing Martyrs’ Day rallies. Two Trinamool Congress (TMC) factions and the Congress will hold separate events within 3 km, vying for ownership of the July 21 commemoration of 1993 police firing victims. CM Mamata Banerjee will lead one rally.

articles.headline: NHRC seeks report on ‘stripping, assault’ of girls in Rajasthan
articles.summary: The National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) took suo motu cognisance of reports alleging girl students in a Rajasthan government school were frisked, beaten, and forced to remove clothes by teachers after ₹1,000 was reported missing. NHRC sought a detailed report on the human rights violation.

articles.headline: Kerala Catholic Church reiterates concerns over FCRA amendment Bill
articles.summary: The Kerala Catholic Bishops’ Council (KCBC) reiterated concerns over the Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Bill, 2026, ahead of its Parliament presentation. KCBC spokesperson Fr. Thomas Tharayil stated existing laws monitor foreign funds and criticized alarming clauses that could confiscate properties built with even partial foreign support.

articles.headline: UCC Bill in M.P. House; uproar over ‘land deals’ linked to CM
articles.summary: The Madhya Pradesh government introduced the draft Uniform Civil Code Bill, prohibiting polygamy and mandating marriage/divorce registration for all faiths. The Congress created an uproar over alleged land purchases linked to CM Mohan Yadav, a charge the BJP denied.

articles.headline: DMK MLA arrested for remarks against Tamil Nadu CM
articles.summary: Thoothukudi District Crime Branch Police arrested Vilathikulam MLA G.V. Markandayan of the DMK for allegedly making "derogatory" remarks against Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay at a public meeting. He was booked under three sections of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita.

articles.headline: Sukhbir Badal appears before SIT in 2015 Punjab firing case
articles.summary: Shiromani Akali Dal (SAD) chief Sukhbir Singh Badal was questioned for two hours by a Punjab police SIT probing the 2015 Behbal Kalan police firing incident. Badal called the SIT a political tool and criticized the AAP government for its alleged failure to apprehend real culprits.

articles.headline: HC orders CID probe into ashram school over sexual assault case
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court’s Kolhapur Circuit Bench transferred the investigation into the 2025 sexual assault of a minor girl at a Ratnagiri ashram school to the State CID. The court expressed concerns over the police's "shoddy investigation" and handling of the case.

articles.headline: Panchayat, local elections to be by Nov. 15: Rajasthan govt.
articles.summary: Rajasthan’s panchayati raj and urban local body elections will be completed by November 15, following the submission of the OBC Political Representation Commission report in August. The State government submitted the schedule to the High Court after strict observations regarding delays.

articles.headline: 30,000 students, 1,100 seats: DU’s 1-year PG rollout hits roadblock
articles.summary: Delhi University's (DU) one-year postgraduate program, designed for FYUP graduates, faces a roadblock with over 30,000 eligible students but only 1,100 seats across 46 disciplines. Students and faculty criticize inadequate planning and unclear communication, prompting protests and a committee for seat expansion.

articles.headline: Suvendu tables police firing report, blames Mamata for ‘inaction’
articles.summary: West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari tabled an inquiry panel report on the 1993 police firing, which killed 13 Youth Congress supporters, in the Assembly. Adhikari blamed Mamata Banerjee for not implementing recommendations or making the report public despite receiving it in 2014.

articles.headline: Kerala double murder case convict sentenced to death
articles.summary: The Palakkad court sentenced Chenthamara to death for the 2025 double murder of Sudhakaran and his mother Lakshmi. The court found the murders premeditated and cited the convict's continued threat to society and lack of remorse, directing ₹20 lakh compensation to victims' children.

articles.headline: Karnataka takes steps to replace 3rd language with AI
articles.summary: Karnataka is implementing Artificial Intelligence (AI) in skill education as a third language option in 1,652 government high schools from 2026-27 under the National Skills Qualifications Framework. A circular was issued for guest AI teachers and school preparations.

articles.headline: Seafarer from Kerala killed in Russian attack near Ukrainian port
articles.summary: Akhil Joyan, a seafarer from Kerala, was killed in a Russian attack on a cargo ship near Ukraine's Odessa port. His family was informed via email. The Chief Minister’s Office initiated efforts to ascertain facts after reports of a Keralite’s death.

articles.headline: Telangana govt. launches Indiramma Housing scheme
articles.summary: The Telangana government launched the Indiramma Housing scheme in the Hyderabad Core Urban Region (CURE) for construction of high-rise buildings. Applications are open July 21-August 10, with a ₹5 lakh government subsidy and ₹6 lakh beneficiary contribution.

articles.headline: Final report on Sabarimala gold ‘theft’ will be filed soon, SIT tells HC
articles.summary: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) informed the Kerala High Court it would file a final report on the alleged Sabarimala gold theft case within 30 days. The investigation includes alleged pilfering from Dwajapalaka idols and stripping salt seized from a Chennai company.

articles.headline: Core sectors grow at five-month high of 5%
articles.summary: India’s core industrial sectors grew 5% in June 2026, a five-month high, driven by a 43.9% rise in iron ore output, now added to the Index of Core Industries (ICI). Electricity, cement, steel, and coal also saw growth, while crude oil, natural gas, refinery products, and fertilisers contracted.

articles.headline: Bengaluru to host India-U.S. adult space group meet in August
articles.summary: Bengaluru will host the next meeting of the India-U.S. Civil Space Joint Working Group (CSJWG) in August. Experts from both countries will hold bilateral talks on space cooperation, highlighting achievements like the NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar (NISAR) mission and Mars 2020 Perseverance Rover.

articles.headline: Court orders release of accused in Nithin Raj case
articles.summary: The Thalassery Principal District Sessions Court ordered the release of Dr. M.K. Ram, the first accused in the abetment to suicide case of BDS student R.L. Nithin Raj. The court cited procedural lapses in his arrest by the Crime Branch.

articles.headline: Delhi HC seeks medical reports of Wangchuk
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court sought all pathological reports of climate activist Sonam Wangchuk from Safdarjung Hospital, AIIMS, and a private lab. His wife sought his transfer to a hospital of his choice. The court prioritised "saving his life first" amidst his protest.

articles.headline: AISA activists end fast on 23rd day after MPs’ appeal
articles.summary: All India Students’ Association (AISA) activists ended their 23-day indefinite hunger strike at Jantar Mantar following appeals from parliamentarians and civil society. They vowed to pursue "other forms of struggle" to address their demands related to examination paper leaks.

articles.headline: Internet cut off for mobile phone users in Central Delhi
articles.summary: Mobile Internet was switched off throughout Central Delhi during the CJP protest, impeding flow of visuals as security forces dispersed demonstrators. The shutdown, similar to a 2021 farmers' agitation blackout, was ordered for 150 sites, initially until noon, then extended to 6 p.m.

articles.headline: Protesters’ anger extends beyond paper leaks
articles.summary: Protesters' anger at the CJP march went beyond exam paper leaks, encompassing perceived government indifference, low salaries, long work hours, inflation, and shut schools. Many BJP voters expressed disillusionment with PM Modi's "arrogance" and the government's failure to address grievances.

articles.headline: Police outnumbered by a sea of marching protesters
articles.summary: Delhi Police were outnumbered by thousands of CJP protesters marching towards Parliament, forcing them to use buses and tear gas to block movement. Despite barricades and an Internet shutdown, the "leaderless" youth group reached Parliament's doorstep, prompting Union Minister J.P. Nadda to meet CJP members.

articles.headline: Monsoon Session off to a stormy start amid Opposition protests
articles.summary: The Lok Sabha faced repeated disruptions on the first day of Parliament's Monsoon Session, adjourned six times and eventually for the day. Opposition MPs protested alleged NEET paper leaks, Ram Temple donation theft, and the Mekedatu dam issue, with Congress, SP, and Trinamool trooping into the Well.

articles.headline: Modi stresses vital role of Parliament, urges MPs to engage in informed debate
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi urged MPs to engage in informed and respectful debate for national progress during the Monsoon Session. He highlighted achievements in space (Skyroot Aerospace, Vikram-1 rocket) and infrastructure (Rajasthan refinery, semiconductor plant), noting India's 7.7% GDP growth despite global conflicts.

articles.headline: Govt. introduces Bill in Lok Sabha to raise strength of judges in SC to 38
articles.summary: The Union government introduced a Bill in Lok Sabha to increase the Supreme Court's sanctioned judge strength from 34 to 38 (excluding CJI) to address mounting case backlog. The move replaces an ordinance, aiming to enable more Constitution Benches and reduce pendency.

articles.headline: RS adjourned six times as Opposition parties seek action in paper leak case
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha was adjourned six times amidst Opposition demands for action in the NEET paper leak case. Nine newly elected members took oath, including three who switched from TMC to BJP. Leader of Opposition Mallikarjun Kharge flagged the issue and police action at Jantar Mantar.

articles.headline: APAAR: SC to direct CBSE to comply with HC order
articles.summary: The Supreme Court agreed to direct CBSE to ensure voluntary consent for the APAAR (Automated Permanent Academic Account Registry) ID, as per an Orissa High Court ruling. The court affirmed the "opt-out/refusal option" in the consent form for parents.

articles.headline: SC refuses to intervene in HC decision on CPV tenders
articles.summary: The Supreme Court refused to intervene against a Delhi High Court decision that quashed tenders for outsourcing Consular, Passport and Visa (CPV) services at four Indian missions abroad. The Centre is free to create a temporary mechanism until a new tender process is complete.

articles.headline: MP seeks transparency in Ram Temple donations
articles.summary: A Samajwadi Party MP sought a direction to the Ram Temple Trust to publish complete financial records, including foreign contributions, digital payments, and inventory of valuables. He argued that devotees deserve transparency regarding the accounting, custody, and utilisation of donations.

articles.headline: Don’t level baseless graft claims against judiciary: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court cautioned against unsubstantiated corruption allegations against judicial officers, stating that social media virality could irreparably "tarnish" a judge’s career. The observation came during a plea challenging a High Court order regarding a contempt case against an activist.

articles.headline: 7 killed in landslide in Jammu and Kashmir as rain toll hits 21
articles.summary: Seven people, including four women and two children, were killed in a landslide in Poonch district, and three others died in shooting-stone incidents in Doda. This brings the rain-related death toll in J&K to 21 in 24 hours. Flash floods also disrupted water and electricity.

articles.headline: Bondada bags NTPC order for 100 MWh BESS
articles.summary: Bondada Engineering secured an order from NTPC Renewable Energy (NTPC REL) to develop a 100 MWh Vanadium Redox Flow Battery Energy Storage System (VRFB BESS) at Khavda Solar Park, Gujarat. Delectrik Systems will be the technology partner for this project.

articles.headline: India joins WTO’s agreement on fisheries subsidies
articles.summary: India joined the WTO’s agreement on fisheries subsidies, which prohibits government support for illegal fishing and over-exploitation. Adopted in 2022, it's the first multilateral WTO agreement with an environmental sustainability objective, focusing on marine wild capture fishing activities.

articles.headline: Insurance firms using ‘ambiguous’, ‘sloppy’ terms to evade liability: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court observed that insurers use "ambiguous" and "sloppy" policy terms to evade or incorrectly assume liabilities, causing policyholders to suffer. The court urged IRDAI to issue a master circular standardizing cross-border coverage clauses, especially for motor insurance, to ensure timely claim disposal.

articles.headline: HDFC Bank stock plunges over 5% on investor concern over earnings
articles.summary: HDFC Bank shares dropped 5.08% on NSE, eroding ₹74,250 crore in shareholder wealth, due to investor concern over its Q1 FY27 performance. Its 5% net profit growth was below market expectation, and net interest margin (NIM) fell to 3.26%, signaling pressure from rising costs and competition.

articles.headline: RBI special swap sees over $20 bn inflow
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India’s (RBI) foreign currency swap facility, utilizing FCNR(B) deposits, ECBs, and OFCBs, has generated over $20 billion in foreign exchange inflows. Operational since June 8, 2026, the facility aims to strengthen India’s balance of payments and incentivize capital inflows.

articles.headline: IOB Q1 net profit climbs 49.3% to ₹1,659 crore
articles.summary: Indian Overseas Bank (IOB) reported a 49.3% rise in net profit to ₹1,659 crore for the June quarter. Net interest income grew 34.3% to ₹3,688 crore, with domestic NIM increasing to 3.48%. Total business expanded 17.7% to ₹6,98,325 crore.

articles.headline: Houthis declare maritime blockade of Saudi Arabia at Red Sea gateway
articles.summary: Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthis announced a maritime embargo of Saudi Arabia, declaring closure of the Bab el-Mandeb strait. This escalates the U.S.-Iran conflict, threatening Saudi Arabia's ability to bypass the Strait of Hormuz and potentially straining global oil markets.

articles.headline: Ukrainian, Russian strikes kill over a dozen civilians
articles.summary: A Ukrainian drone strike killed five, including a child, in Russia’s Belgorod region, while Russian strikes killed eight across Ukrainian cities including Pavlograd, Kramatorsk, Odesa, and Zaporizhzhia. Both nations deny targeting civilians but have intensified retaliatory attacks amidst stalled peace talks.

articles.headline: China, Philippines trade barbs in South China Sea encounter
articles.summary: The Philippine military accused China’s coast guard of injuring a Filipino sailor with a wooden baton and damaging his vessel during an incident in the disputed Second Thomas Shoal. China's coast guard countered, claiming Filipino personnel attacked their boat.

articles.headline: Thousands of Israelis march seeking return of settlements in Gaza Strip
articles.summary: Thousands of Israelis, including settlers and right-wing activists, marched towards the Gaza border, demanding the government re-establish Israeli settlements in the war-shattered Palestinian territory. This push follows the 2005 withdrawal, which participants called temporary, despite much of Gaza being uninhabitable.

articles.headline: Chess legend Judit Polgar declines post of Hungary President
articles.summary: Chess legend Judit Polgar declined Hungarian Prime Minister Peter Magyar’s offer to become the country's President. Polgar stated she lacked the strength to unite a divided nation, despite Magyar's "regime change" push against Viktor Orban. President Tamas Sulyok was recently removed by amendment.

articles.headline: Sathiyan keeps Delhi’s hopes alive
articles.summary: Captain G. Sathiyan's clean sweep of Payas Jain helped Dabang Delhi TTC secure a hard-fought 9-6 victory over Ahmedabad, keeping their Ultimate Table Tennis (UTT) Season 7 semifinal hopes alive. He overcame a 10-6 deficit in the first game to snatch the opener.

articles.headline: A sad story: Sharath on TT’s CWG omission
articles.summary: A. Sharath Kamal called the exclusion of table tennis from the 2026 Commonwealth Games a "sad story" and a major setback for Indian athletes. He highlighted TT's success in previous CWG editions, where India won 28 medals, including 10 gold.

articles.headline: Torres the hero as Spanish Armada sinks Argentina
articles.summary: Ferran Torres scored in extra-time, securing Spain's 1-0 victory over Argentina in the World Cup final, their second global crown. Spain dominated possession and stifled Lionel Messi, who managed only 15 touches in the first half. Emiliano Martínez's heroics delayed the inevitable for Argentina.

articles.headline: Spain was better and we accept it, says Argentina coach Scaloni
articles.summary: Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni conceded Spain deserved its 1-0 World Cup final victory, expressing sadness but pride in his players' effort. He acknowledged Spain's superiority and highlighted Argentina's ability to "lose, and we accept it." Scaloni also praised Lionel Messi's enduring brilliance.

articles.headline: A legend built without its decorated kings
articles.summary: Spain's World Cup victory was driven by players from various LaLiga clubs, especially Barcelona (9 players), showcasing a legend built without Real Madrid players. Marc Cucurella’s move to Real further highlighted this absence, revealing a triumph of diverse Spanish football talent.

articles.headline: Poor show by bowlers and fitness issues spell trouble
articles.summary: India's cricket team faces pressing problems with poor bowling and fitness issues, as highlighted by a 27-run defeat against England. Key pacers like Jasprit Bumrah are injured, while others struggle with fitness. The team's reluctance to include seasoned pacers and keep spinner Kuldeep Yadav out is hurting performance.

articles.headline: U Mumba TT emerges UTT Juniors champion
articles.summary: U Mumba TT defeated Dabang Delhi TT 5-2 to clinch the Ultimate Table Tennis (UTT) Juniors championship in Panaji. Ananya started with a win, and Shubham and Ahona secured the mixed doubles. Ahona then sealed the win by brushing aside Sreejani.

articles.headline: Sports Ministry details Commonwealth Games preparations
articles.summary: The Sports Ministry reported spending ₹53.09 crore on training and exposure, with ₹20 crore for India's 32-member athletics contingent for the Commonwealth Games in Glasgow. Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya also stated India had 78 doping cases this year, leading to bans.

articles.headline: Richa among Bengal probables for forthcoming season
articles.summary: World Cup winner Richa Ghosh is among 28 probables named by the Cricket Association of Bengal for the State women’s team for the 2026-27 season. The list includes several other prominent players.

articles.headline: Anand believes Gukesh can turn things around
articles.summary: Five-time world champion Viswanathan Anand believes D. Gukesh can overcome his recent patchy form before defending his World title against Javokhir Sindarov. Anand noted Gukesh's recent struggles but expressed hope for a turnaround, possibly inspired by the upcoming Chess Olympiad.

articles.headline: Aston Villa signs Brazil midfielder Gomes
articles.summary: Aston Villa signed Brazil midfielder Joao Gomes from Wolves for a reported £38 million. Gomes was also targeted by Atletico Madrid, but Villa secured the 25-year-old after Chelsea agreed to sign Morgan Rogers.

articles.headline: Garcia has left as Belgium coach, says federation
articles.summary: Rudi Garcia has left his role as Belgium coach after the team was knocked out of the World Cup quarterfinals by Spain. The Frenchman took over in January 2025.

articles.headline: Silencing the doubters, the Ro-Hit way
articles.summary: The article discusses Rohit Sharma's consistent performance despite being questioned and "summarily axed" as captain. It highlights his 50-plus scores in 50% of his ODIs since March last year and criticizes unwarranted scrutiny, emphasizing his enduring class and fitness.

articles.headline: India’s green transition is missing long-duration energy storage
articles.summary: India's energy storage roadmap needs long-duration energy storage (LDES) technologies to ensure grid reliability during prolonged low solar/wind generation. LDES, offering days-long power, is crucial for India's renewable-heavy grid, but current plans focus on shorter durations. Technology-agnostic incentives and policy integration are vital.

articles.headline: Security meets ecology as BSF mulls fence in Sundarbans
articles.summary: The BSF is conducting a feasibility study to install physical fences along a 90-km stretch of the Sundarbans mangroves on the Indo-Bangladesh border to deter illegal activities. Scientists express concern that this could harm biodiversity and displace vulnerable populations, though BSF claims structures will not affect wildlife.

articles.headline: Noida BJP Leader Up As Students Protest Sparks Clash
articles.summary: A BJP leader's convoy was allegedly attacked by students protesting the new education policy near Chaudhary Charan Singh University, Meerut. The incident led to clashes and police intervention. The leader claimed students were instigated, while students alleged BJP workers assaulted them.

articles.headline: UP Polls Next Year: LS on Opening Day; 5th to Conclude
articles.summary: Parliament's monsoon session is expected to begin on July 20 and conclude on August 10. The government plans to introduce 20 bills. Opposition leaders want a discussion on issues like price rise, fuel hike, and unemployment.

articles.headline: Vijay Singh to Exit Tata Trusts, Wants Ends
articles.summary: Vijay Singh, vice chairman of Tata Trusts, has indicated he wishes to exit the Trusts to focus on other pursuits. He was a key figure during Ratan Tata's tenure and the Mistry ouster.

articles.headline: $21 Billion Streams in via Incentive Window
articles.summary: India's overseas direct investment (ODI) under the incentive window saw $21.3 billion inflows between FY18-FY23. The data was released by RBI, showing a surge in FY22 and FY23 compared to FY18-FY21, indicating increased utilization of the scheme.

articles.headline: Private Lenders Draw Thinner Margins on Tepid Credit Growth
articles.summary: Private sector banks' net interest margins (NIMs) are expected to shrink due to slow credit growth and increased deposit costs. Data shows lower NIMs for several banks like Axis Bank and HDFC Bank. Public sector banks show mixed trends.

articles.headline: Qcomm Leverages Cart to Draw Higher Margins from Cos
articles.summary: Qualcomm is increasing its charges for patent usage from consumer electronics companies, including smartphone makers like Xiaomi and Oppo. This move aims to boost revenue amid rising R&D costs and increasing chip prices.

articles.headline: Pilots Seek a Stop to W Asia Flights
articles.summary: Pilots' associations are urging the government to restrict flights to West Asia due to high temperatures and potential health risks. This follows recent incidents of pilots falling ill due to extreme heat in the region.

articles.headline: Deeptech Gains Aim to Achieve Escape Velocity with Skyroot Thrust
articles.summary: Deeptech startups, particularly in aerospace like Skyroot Aerospace, are attracting significant venture capital investments. The sector's funding has surged, indicating a shift towards advanced technologies. Investors see potential for high growth.

articles.headline: No LTCG Cap for Domestic Investors
articles.summary: The government has decided not to impose a long-term capital gains (LTCG) tax cap for domestic investors, aiming to boost capital market participation. This decision was influenced by market feedback against such a cap.

articles.headline: NABFID to Fund Infra Via Spl Bonds
articles.summary: The National Bank for Financing Infrastructure and Development (NABFID) plans to raise ₹20,000 crore through special infrastructure bonds. This is part of its strategy to fund large-scale infrastructure projects across India.

articles.headline: Core Output High in June, Base Effect Key
articles.summary: India's eight core sectors' output grew 8.2% in June, driven by a favorable base effect from last year's low growth. Coal, steel, and electricity sectors showed strong performance, indicating industrial recovery.

articles.headline: TCS Acquires Land in Vizag, Pune for OpenAI Data Sites
articles.summary: TCS has acquired large land parcels in Visakhapatnam and Pune to build state-of-the-art data centers, enhancing its capabilities for AI and other advanced technologies. This move is strategic for its global operations.

articles.headline: CJP March Sparks Clashes; Calm After Nadda Meet
articles.summary: The SCM JP March to Parliament on July 20 saw clashes between BJP Yuva Morcha workers and police. Protesters were demanding the resignation of the Chief Minister over alleged corruption. Later, BJP President J.P. Nadda met with workers to calm the situation.

articles.headline: Oppn Stalls LS on Opening Day; Protests Likely to Continue
articles.summary: Opposition parties stalled the Lok Sabha on the opening day of the monsoon session over demands for discussions on Manipur violence. Protests are expected to continue, disrupting parliamentary proceedings for several days.

articles.headline: Sharing With Modi: Shah Holds Four-hour Meeting With Pradhan
articles.summary: Home Minister Amit Shah held a four-hour meeting with Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, reportedly to discuss political strategies ahead of upcoming elections and current government policies.

articles.headline: Unity Show Masks Deep Opposition Cracks; DMK and AAP Skip Meet
articles.summary: Opposition parties showed a united front in Parliament but deep cracks remain, with DMK and AAP skipping a crucial meeting. This disunity highlights challenges in forming a cohesive opposition alliance against the ruling party.

articles.headline: Ayodhya Temple Funds Case: Supreme Court Cautions Against Politicising Issue
articles.summary: The Supreme Court cautioned against politicizing the Ayodhya temple funds case. It directed a detailed report on the utilization of funds and maintenance, emphasizing that the focus should be on accountability, not political blame games.

articles.headline: SC Seeks Opt-Out Option in CBSE APAAR Consent Forms
articles.summary: The Supreme Court questioned CBSE's consent forms for the APAAR ID program, seeking an opt-out option for parents. The program aims to create a unique ID for students, but raises privacy concerns.

articles.headline: DKS Launches PRC Drive to Step Up Voter Enrolment
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar launched a drive to enroll 18-year-olds and update voter lists, aiming to increase participation. This initiative follows the Election Commission's directives to ensure accurate voter rolls.

articles.headline: High Court Questions SEC's Parallel Voter Roll Revision
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh High Court questioned the State Election Commission's parallel voter roll revision exercise, directing it to clarify its process. The court is investigating potential irregularities in voter list updates.

articles.headline: WB Mamata Benga in Govt Office
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee attended government offices, urging employees to be punctual. This is part of her initiative to improve governance and accountability in the state administration.

articles.headline: UltraTech Comes Up with a Record Quarter; Profit Surges 17%
articles.summary: UltraTech Cement reported a 17% rise in standalone net profit to ₹1,698 crore for the June quarter. Revenue grew 18% to ₹18,016 crore, driven by strong demand and operational efficiency.

articles.headline: Godrej Agro to Produce More as Edible Oil Prices Stay High
articles.summary: Godrej Agrovet plans to increase palm oil production in India to reduce reliance on imports, capitalizing on high edible oil prices. The company aims to expand cultivation and processing capacity.

articles.headline: Pilots' Body Seeks Rule to Highlight Duty Restrictions
articles.summary: The Pilots' Federation of India (PFI) has urged the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) to implement rules that highlight duty restrictions for pilots, especially concerning excessive work hours and fatigue.

articles.headline: Early Pack Up
articles.summary: Early Pack Up, a startup creating custom packaging solutions, is gaining traction with its innovative approach. The company focuses on sustainable and creative packaging for e-commerce businesses.

articles.headline: Different Route
articles.summary: Different Route is a startup specializing in alternative travel experiences. It designs unique itineraries for offbeat destinations, catering to a growing market of experiential travelers.

articles.headline: Westside Plans To Grow 3X In Metro
articles.summary: Westside, a lifestyle retail chain, plans to triple its footprint in metro cities. The expansion strategy includes opening larger format stores and enhancing its online presence to cater to urban consumers.

articles.headline: The New Cup That Cheers A Bit More
articles.summary: Specialty tea brand Teabox is seeing growth, appealing to younger, Gen Z consumers with innovative blends and marketing. The company highlights the shift in consumer preferences towards premium, healthy, and ethical tea options.

articles.headline: Low-Purchasing Power in Villages Seen as a Creator
articles.summary: Low purchasing power in villages is driving innovation in marketing and product development. Companies are adapting strategies to cater to rural consumers by offering affordable and necessity-driven products.

articles.headline: Not Just Office Large Cos Opt for Premium Workplace Too
articles.summary: Large companies are increasingly opting for premium, flexible co-working spaces and managed offices, moving beyond traditional offices. This trend is driven by demand for better infrastructure, amenities, and cost-efficiency.

articles.headline: Right Policy Space for Strategic Industries
articles.summary: India needs clear policies and funding to boost strategic industries like space and defence. The article discusses the importance of indigenization, private sector participation, and export potential for these critical sectors.

articles.headline: Energy Shock vs AI Boom
articles.summary: The article discusses the impact of energy shocks on global economies and contrasts it with the potential economic boom from Artificial Intelligence. It explores how AI might mitigate energy crises or create new opportunities.

articles.headline: Expressed Universe
articles.summary: This article discusses how humans perceive and express the universe. It delves into philosophical and scientific perspectives on consciousness, reality, and the limits of human understanding.

articles.headline: Stop Romanticising India's Cattle Class
articles.summary: The article criticizes the poor treatment of passengers, dubbed 'cattle class,' on Indian railways and airlines. It calls for improved infrastructure, better services, and greater respect for ordinary citizens.

articles.headline: Don't Put a Toll on UPI
articles.summary: The article argues against imposing a toll or charges on UPI transactions, emphasizing its role in digital payments and financial inclusion. It highlights the potential negative impact on adoption and the economy.

articles.headline: Cranking Up the Rank Already
articles.summary: This article discusses India's efforts to improve its global economic rankings. It highlights policy reforms, infrastructure development, and ease of doing business initiatives aimed at boosting competitiveness.

articles.headline: With the World Cup done, we can Return to Our Circadian Rhythm
articles.summary: This humorous piece reflects on the return to normal daily routines after the World Cup excitement, noting the disruption to sleep and work schedules.

articles.headline: Silence in Northeast
articles.summary: The article criticizes the lack of national media attention and political discourse on issues in India's Northeast, highlighting a disparity compared to other regions. It calls for greater engagement and understanding of the region's challenges.

articles.headline: Crude Prices Might Fall If Refining Output in Countries Rebuild
articles.summary: Global crude oil prices could fall if major refining countries rebuild their output capacity, easing supply constraints. Geopolitical tensions and inventory levels remain key factors influencing price stability.

articles.headline: Takeda Gets DGCI Nod for India's 1st Dengue Vaccine
articles.summary: Takeda Pharmaceutical India received approval from the Drugs Controller General of India (DGCI) for its dengue vaccine, Qdenga. This marks the first approved dengue vaccine in India, offering a new public health tool.

articles.headline: Sobha Plans to Raise Up to ₹1K Cr Through NCDs
articles.summary: Sobha Ltd. plans to raise up to ₹1,000 crore through the issuance of non-convertible debentures (NCDs). The funds will be used for business expansion, debt refinancing, and general corporate purposes.

articles.headline: Costlier Fodder Feeds Protein Inflation in June
articles.summary: Protein inflation rose in June, driven by increased prices of fodder and animal feed. This impacts the cost of milk, eggs, and meat, contributing to overall food inflation.

articles.headline: Lastly LNG, Declining Domestic Output Lower Gas Consumption
articles.summary: India's gas consumption decreased due to lower LNG imports and declining domestic production. This trend is impacting industrial sectors reliant on natural gas, with implications for energy security.

articles.headline: Lal Salaaaam
articles.summary: Lionel Messi and Argentina are celebrating their World Cup victory. The article reviews key moments of the tournament, highlighting Messi's leadership and the team's performance.

articles.headline: SC Gives Parsvnath Final Week to Pay, Warns of Jail
articles.summary: The Supreme Court gave Parsvnath Developers a final week to pay ₹12 crore to homebuyers, warning of jail time for directors if they fail to comply. The case highlights issues in real estate and consumer protection.

articles.headline: Romal Shetty Gets Seco Term as CEO of Deloitte India
articles.summary: Romal Shetty has been reappointed as CEO of Deloitte India for a second term, recognizing his leadership and contributions to the firm's growth and strategy in the region.

articles.headline: Colleges Rushing to Embed Edtech AI Modules to Get Wards Job-ready
articles.summary: Colleges are rapidly integrating AI modules and edtech solutions into their curricula to better prepare students for the job market. This move aims to bridge the skill gap and enhance employability in emerging tech sectors.

articles.headline: Paytm's Q1 Net Profit Up 79% to ₹220Cr, Revenue Rises 28%
articles.summary: Paytm reported a 79% rise in net profit to ₹220 crore and a 28% increase in revenue for Q1 FY24. The strong financial performance is driven by growth in payment services and merchant base expansion.

articles.headline: Norwest and Skeegen Lead ₹40M Round in Raghu Vamsi Aero
articles.summary: Norwest Venture Partners and Skeegen Capital led a $40 million funding round in Raghu Vamsi Aerospace, a startup focused on advanced aerospace manufacturing and MRO services.

articles.headline: Operator-Led Startups get Funding: Report
articles.summary: Operator-led startups, founded by experienced industry professionals, are increasingly attracting venture capital funding. These founders bring deep domain expertise, enhancing their chances of success.

articles.headline: Cream of Crop
articles.summary: Bengaluru's top engineering colleges have a high placement rate for their students in leading technology companies. This highlights the strong talent pool and industry demand for skilled graduates.

articles.headline: Alibaba Had $250 Million Yuan for Illegal Sales
articles.summary: Alibaba was fined 250 million yuan (equivalent to $34.5 million) by Chinese regulators for illegal sales practices. This includes offering incentives to merchants for exclusive platform use.

articles.headline: Bezos, Others Back CuspAI's $450M Round
articles.summary: Jeff Bezos and other investors participated in CuspAI's $450 million funding round. The startup focuses on AI-driven drug discovery and materials science, attracting significant investment from prominent figures.

articles.headline: Hackers Are Playing the Long Game With Critical Infrastructure
articles.summary: Hackers are increasingly targeting critical infrastructure with sophisticated, long-term attacks. Experts warn of persistent threats from state-sponsored actors aiming to disrupt essential services and steal data over extended periods.

articles.headline: Talent Depth Keeps India Ahead as GCC Hub
articles.summary: India's deep talent pool and favorable ecosystem are keeping it ahead as a Global Capability Center (GCC) hub. Bengaluru, Hyderabad, and Pune are key cities attracting significant investment in GCCs.

articles.headline: Rupee Slips to 2-Month Low on RBI Concerns
articles.summary: The Indian Rupee slipped to a two-month low against the US dollar amid concerns about RBI's intervention. Rising crude prices and foreign fund outflows contributed to the depreciation.

articles.headline: Japan's SOMPO Seeks Control of Universal Organics
articles.summary: Japan's SOMPO Holdings is seeking to acquire majority control of Universal Organics, a leading Indian agrochemical company. This move signals increasing foreign investment in India's agriculture sector.

articles.headline: Pvt Banks Go for a Tumble on D-St After Q1 Stumble
articles.summary: Private sector banks' shares tumbled on Dalal Street after reporting weaker-than-expected Q1 earnings. Concerns about asset quality and slower credit growth weighed on investor sentiment.

articles.headline: SBI Funds Managers' Investors Line in for 10-15% Listing Pop
articles.summary: Investors are queuing up for SBI Funds Management's IPO, expecting a 10-15% listing pop. Strong demand is driven by the brand's reputation and growth prospects in the asset management industry.

articles.headline: Wall St Steadies as AI Swells, Discover Some of AI's Next
articles.summary: Wall Street is stabilizing amidst the AI boom, with investors actively seeking the next wave of AI-driven companies beyond the current leaders. The focus is on identifying undervalued opportunities in the AI ecosystem.

articles.headline: RBI Easy Goes on ₹30,000-Cr Bond
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India conducted an easy bond auction for ₹30,000 crore, indicating comfortable market liquidity. This helps manage government borrowing and stabilize interest rates.

articles.headline: AMC Pays Very Little in Fees to Investment Managers
articles.summary: Asset management companies (AMCs) pay a small fraction of their fees to investment managers, raising concerns about remuneration structures. This disparity can impact talent retention and performance.

articles.headline: War Pushes Oil Refiners to Be Nimble in Europe
articles.summary: The Russia-Ukraine war has forced European oil refiners to adapt quickly, diversifying supply chains and adjusting to new geopolitical realities. This has led to shifts in crude sourcing and product flows.

articles.headline: Get Timing Wrong Even a Bit, Take a Hit on Returns
articles.summary: Mis-timing market entry or exit can significantly impact investment returns. Even minor errors in timing can lead to substantial losses, emphasizing the importance of long-term strategies over short-term speculation.

articles.headline: Can Wall St Survive Kind of Korean Stock Volatility?
articles.summary: The article discusses whether Wall Street could withstand the kind of extreme stock market volatility recently seen in South Korea. It compares market structures and investor behavior, highlighting potential contagion risks.

articles.headline: FCNR Scheme: Withholding Tax Fear Haunts US, Singapore NRIs
articles.summary: Non-resident Indians (NRIs) in the US and Singapore are hesitant to invest in India's Foreign Currency Non-Resident (FCNR) deposit scheme due to concerns about withholding tax. This impacts capital inflows.

articles.headline: Intermediary (FCNR-B) Funds to Temporarily Bridge Gap
articles.summary: Intermediary FCNR-B funds are being used to temporarily bridge the gap in liquidity, allowing banks to raise foreign currency deposits. This mechanism helps manage short-term funding needs.

articles.headline: Indian Bonds Stead After Merger with Crude Oil
articles.summary: Indian bonds showed stability despite the merger news and rising crude oil prices. This resilience is attributed to strong domestic fundamentals and RBI's supportive policies.

articles.headline: Karur Vysya Bank Q1 Net Jumps 45%
articles.summary: Karur Vysya Bank reported a 45% increase in net profit for Q1, driven by higher net interest income and improved asset quality. The bank's financial health remains strong.

articles.headline: Demonstration of Capability Yields at a High
articles.summary: The article discusses how demonstrating strong capabilities and innovation helps companies achieve high yields and market success. This is crucial for attracting investment and sustaining growth.

articles.headline: Auction-style Bidding
articles.summary: Auction-style bidding is becoming prevalent in various sectors, from government contracts to online marketplaces. This method ensures competitive pricing and transparency.

articles.headline: $26B Expected by July-end
articles.summary: The government expects to raise $26 billion by July-end through various market borrowings and disinvestment. This aims to manage fiscal deficit and fund public expenditure.

articles.headline: Private Sector
articles.summary: The private sector is playing a crucial role in India's economic growth, driven by investments in manufacturing, services, and technology. Policy support is further enhancing its contribution.

articles.headline: Algorithms Now Help Venture AI
articles.summary: Venture capital firms are increasingly using AI algorithms to identify promising startups and optimize investment decisions. This data-driven approach enhances efficiency and reduces risks in the investment process.

articles.headline: The Good
articles.summary: Over 250,000 developers worldwide are actively contributing to AI open-source projects. This collaboration accelerates AI innovation and democratizes access to advanced technologies.

articles.headline: The Bad
articles.summary: A significant percentage of businesses struggle to fully realize the benefits of AI implementations, often due to data quality issues, integration challenges, and lack of skilled personnel.

articles.headline: The Ugly
articles.summary: Ethical concerns, biases, and regulatory gaps in AI development pose significant challenges. Ensuring fairness, transparency, and accountability is crucial for responsible AI adoption.

articles.headline: The High Cost of AI Revolution
articles.summary: The AI revolution comes with high costs, including significant investments in infrastructure, talent, and energy. This poses challenges for companies and nations seeking to adopt AI widely.

articles.headline: The Micro-Pricing War Reshaping AI Battle
articles.summary: A micro-pricing war is emerging in the AI market, with companies competing on per-query or per-use costs for AI services. This competition is driving down prices and increasing accessibility.

articles.headline: AI is High on Promise, Low on Trust
articles.summary: Despite AI's immense potential, public trust remains low due to concerns about bias, privacy, and job displacement. Addressing these ethical challenges is crucial for widespread AI adoption and acceptance.

articles.headline: A Pre-Flight Check That Your Pitch Deck Forgot?
articles.summary: This article offers advice to startups on common pitch deck omissions, particularly regarding market fit, unit economics, and team capabilities. It stresses thorough preparation for investor engagement.

articles.headline: Iron Ore, Cement, Power Output Propels June Core Growth to 5%
articles.summary: India's core infrastructure sector output grew 5% in June, driven by strong performance in iron ore, cement, and electricity. This indicates a sustained recovery in industrial activity.

articles.headline: NABFID Set to Raise ₹20,000 cr Through Zero-Coupon Bonds
articles.summary: NABFID plans to raise ₹20,000 crore via ten-year zero-coupon bonds to fund long-term infrastructure projects. This innovative financing aims to attract diverse investors.

articles.headline: CBI Seeks Single-window GST Administration for Multi-state Cos
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) is working towards a single-window GST administration for companies operating in multiple states. This aims to streamline compliance and reduce complexities.

articles.headline: No Plans To Scrap GST on LPG: Govt To Lok Sabha
articles.summary: The government clarified in Lok Sabha that there are no plans to scrap GST on petroleum products, including LPG. This statement addresses concerns about tax burden on essential commodities.

articles.headline: Global Market Trends and Investor Sentiment Analyzed
articles.summary: Analysis of global market trends and investor sentiment reveals shifts in international economic factors, commodity prices, and geopolitical influences on trade and finance. Updates on market indices, stock movements, and investor confidence are unreadable.

articles.headline: Healthcare Sector Sees Innovation and Investment
articles.summary: The healthcare sector is experiencing innovation and investment, with potential developments in medical technologies, pharmaceuticals, and funding initiatives. Specifics on new projects, public or private funding, and improvements to medical services remain unreadable.

articles.headline: Education Sector Undergoes Policy Reforms
articles.summary: Reforms are being implemented in the education sector, potentially addressing curriculum changes, funding models, and policies to enhance educational outcomes and access. Specific details on policy shifts or expected impacts are unreadable.

articles.headline: Cultural Heritage Promotion and Preservation Efforts Underway

articles.headline: Vijay Singh's Future on Sir Ratan Tata Trust Board Uncertain
articles.summary: Vijay Singh's reappointment to the Sir Ratan Tata Trust board is in doubt due to a Maharashtra charity commissioner order preventing meetings. His earlier stance on Tata Sons' listing also impacts his chances.

articles.headline: India Approves First Dengue Vaccine QDENGA for Ages 4-60
articles.summary: India's DCGI approved Takeda's QDENGA, the first dengue vaccine, for ages 4-60. Administered in two doses, it shows 80.2% efficacy against dengue and 90.4% against hospitalization, regardless of prior infection. Launch expected in 2027.

articles.headline: Transition VC Raises ₹1,500 Crore for Energy Sector Fund
articles.summary: Energy-focused VC fund Transition VC is raising ₹1,500 crore for its second fund, targeting battery storage, energy networks, thermal energy storage, advanced materials, and geothermal energy to support India's energy hardware sector.

articles.headline: Houthis Blockade Saudi Arabia, Widening Red Sea Threat
articles.summary: Yemen's Iran-aligned Houthis declared a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, escalating threats to global energy supplies and trade beyond the Gulf. This move could potentially impact oil supply significantly if key straits are affected.

articles.headline: Four Indian Seafarers Killed in Ship Attack Near Odesa
articles.summary: Four Indian seafarers died and one was injured in a ship attack near Odesa, Ukraine. India condemned the targeting of commercial shipping and civilian crews, urging the avoidance of such actions.

articles.headline: Singapore Airlines to Consider Air India Funding Requests Cautiously
articles.summary: Singapore Airlines (SIA) will "carefully consider" additional capital requests from Air India, reaffirming its commitment but prioritizing prudent allocation. Investor concerns exist over Air India's mounting losses and headwinds.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Urges Against Politicizing CBI, ED Probes
articles.summary: The Supreme Court advised against politicizing CBI and ED investigations, emphasizing the importance of independent agencies and cautioning against undermining their credibility amid accusations of bias.

articles.headline: Six Killed in Ethanol Tanker Chain Collision and Fire
articles.summary: Six people died in a highway accident involving an ethanol-laden tanker, causing a chain collision and fire. The incident highlights the dangers of transporting flammable materials, with investigations ongoing.

articles.headline: Trump Warns Iran of Retaliation for US Troop Deaths
articles.summary: President Trump warned Iran it would pay "many times over" for any US troop deaths, following recent fatalities. He also defended Israeli PM Netanyahu against alleged war crimes.

articles.headline: India Indicates Indus Water Treaty Needs Modification
articles.summary: India has signaled that the Indus Water Treaty requires modifications to continue functioning, amidst ongoing water-sharing disputes with Pakistan. The existing framework may no longer be suitable.

articles.headline: ONGC CSR Funds to RSS-linked Bodies Questioned
articles.summary: Questions have been raised regarding Oil and Natural Gas Corporation's (ONGC) CSR funds allegedly flowing to RSS-linked organizations. Calls for transparency in the use of public resources have been made.

articles.headline: Karnataka's Free Residency Certificates Spark Political Row
articles.summary: Karnataka's initiative to distribute free residency certificates is facing criticism, with opposition parties alleging political motives to influence elections. The government defends it as a welfare measure.

articles.headline: DMK MLA Arrested for Threatening Remarks Against CM
articles.summary: A DMK MLA was arrested for making derogatory and threatening speeches against Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay, marking the second such arrest of a DMK MLA for inflammatory remarks.

articles.headline: High Court Rules on Transfer of Funds to Frozen Accounts
articles.summary: The High Court will frame rules for transferring funds to three frozen accounts, likely in relation to a financial dispute. This directive aims for a transparent process for managing the accounts.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Delhi HC Quashing Visa Service Tenders
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the Delhi High Court's decision to quash tenders for passport, visa, and consular services overseas, requiring the MEA to re-evaluate its procurement processes.

articles.headline: Tripura DGP Found Dead in Office; Magisterial Probe Ordered
articles.summary: Tripura DGP Anurag Dhankar was found dead in his Agartala office washroom. A magisterial probe has been ordered, with police suspecting suicide but not confirming details or a death note.

articles.headline: JD Vance's Fourth Child Born, First for Sitting US VP in 150 Years
articles.summary: US Vice President JD Vance and his wife Usha welcomed their fourth child, Alec Neel Vance, making him the first sitting VP in over 150 years to have a baby in office. Vance advocates for larger families.

articles.headline: Karnataka Launches Doorstep Delivery of Caste, Residence Certificates
articles.summary: Karnataka's CM launched free doorstep delivery of Permanent Residence Certificates (PRCs) for voter verification and caste certificates, aiming to simplify access to government services and support electoral roll revisions.

articles.headline: Supreme Court to Supervise SIT Probe into Ram Temple Fund Theft
articles.summary: The Supreme Court will oversee the investigation into Ayodhya Ram Mandir fund embezzlement, ordering a senior IPS-led SIT to take over and directing the Trust to preserve donation receipts.

articles.headline: Mumbai Court Sentences 44 Somali Pirates to Life Imprisonment
articles.summary: A Mumbai special court handed life sentences to 44 Somali pirates for piracy, marking the first conviction under the Maritime Anti-Piracy Act, 2022. Their arrests followed Indian Navy operations.

articles.headline: Andy Burnham Becomes UK Prime Minister, Vows 10-Year Plan
articles.summary: Andy Burnham is the 7th UK PM in 10 years, promising a 10-year plan for stability and economic revival, prioritizing essential needs and a new economic model. He aims to end street sleeping.

articles.headline: High Court: Father's Education Duty Extends to Adult Daughter
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court ruled a father must fund his adult unmarried daughter's education, including postgraduate studies, upholding an order for ₹16 lakh for an MD course.

articles.headline: HC Dismisses PIL Against Bengaluru Township Land Acquisition
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court dismissed a PIL challenging the acquisition of 516 acres for the Greater Bengaluru Integrated Township project near Bidadi, finding no legal violations in the process.

articles.headline: NTA Rebuts Claims of Forged NEET-UG OMR Sheets
articles.summary: The National Testing Agency (NTA) refuted allegations of forged NEET-UG OMR sheets, stating digitally altered or AI-generated images were circulated, warning candidates of severe action for fabricated records.

articles.headline: Government Rules Out Ethanol Blending Beyond 20% for Now
articles.summary: The government has made no decision to increase ethanol blending in petrol beyond 20% or offer pure petrol, stating future decisions will depend on scientific studies and consultations.

articles.headline: Rahul Gandhi Calls Modi 'Most Anti-Youth PM'
articles.summary: Rahul Gandhi criticized PM Narendra Modi as the 'most anti-youth PM India has had,' condemning police action against protesters and urging acceptance of legitimate demands.

articles.headline: Kerala Man Sentenced to Death for Three Murders
articles.summary: A Kerala man received the death penalty for hacking a neighbour and her son to death, having previously killed the son's wife while out on bail. The court deemed the crimes 'rarest of rare'.

articles.headline: IndiGo Places Record Order for Over 1,000 Aircraft Engines
articles.summary: IndiGo ordered over 1,000 LEAP-1A engines from CFM International for its Airbus A320neo fleet, a deal worth $14.5 billion. CFM will also establish an engine MRO facility in India.

articles.headline: Luis de la Fuente Leads Spain to World Cup Triumph
articles.summary: Luis de la Fuente orchestrated Spain's revival, leading them to European and World Cup titles by leveraging his experience with youth teams and fostering collective spirit.

articles.headline: Kevin Keegan, Two-Time Ballon d'Or Winner, Dies at 75
articles.summary: Former Liverpool and England star Kevin Keegan, a two-time Ballon d'Or winner, has died at 75 after battling cancer. He had a decorated career with multiple titles and later managed England.

articles.headline: New Zealand Secures Thrilling One-Wicket Win Over West Indies
articles.summary: Mark Chapman scored 80 and Mitchell Santner guided New Zealand to a tense one-wicket victory against West Indies, securing an unassailable 3-1 lead in the ODI series.

articles.headline: Kushagra Ojha's Unbeaten Century Powers India U-19
articles.summary: Kushagra Ojha scored an unbeaten 138 to help India U-19 reach 253 for three against Sri Lanka on Day 1 of the second Youth Test in Colombo.

articles.headline: Arjun Erigaisi Beats World Champion Gukesh in Chennai Chess Meet
articles.summary: Arjun Erigaisi secured a victory over world champion D. Gukesh in a tense game at the Chennai GM chess meet, joining Alireza Firouzja at the top of the standings.

articles.headline: NC Protests in Delhi for J&K Statehood Restoration
articles.summary: National Conference leaders, including Farooq and Omar Abdullah, protested in Delhi demanding immediate restoration of Jammu and Kashmir's Statehood, facing police action amid other demonstrations.

articles.headline: Indo MIM Sets IPO Price Band at ₹461-₹485
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based Indo MIM Ltd. set its ₹3,812 crore IPO price band at ₹461-₹485 per share, with the IPO opening from July 23-27 to fund loan repayment and offer sales.

articles.headline: Noel Tata Responds on Share Transfer Case
articles.summary: Noel Tata stated that the challan for his son-in-law's transfer case was processed on time, responding to the Delhi L-G's inquiry into the investigation delay.

articles.headline: India's Global Captive Centers Reach 1,833
articles.summary: India's global captive centers reached 1,833 in H1 2023, adding 126 new centers, indicating its growing role as a hub for shared services and technology operations.
